verses.kjv: So when they had rowed about five and twenty or thirty furlongs, they see Jesus walking on the sea, and drawing nigh unto the ship: and they were afraid.
verses.bsb: When they had rowed about three or four miles, they saw Jesus approaching the boat, walking on the sea—and they were terrified.

verses.esv: But he said to them, “It is I; do not be afraid.”
verses.kjv: But he saith unto them, It is I; be not afraid.
verses.bsb: But Jesus spoke up: “It is I; do not be afraid.”

verses.esv: Then they were glad to take him into the boat, and immediately the boat was at the land to which they were going.
verses.kjv: Then they willingly received him into the ship: and immediately the ship was at the land whither they went.
verses.bsb: Then they were willing to take Him into the boat, and at once the boat reached the shore where they were heading.

verses.esv: On the next day the crowd that remained on the other side of the sea saw that there had been only one boat there, and that Jesus had not entered the boat with his disciples, but that his disciples had gone away alone.
verses.kjv: ¶ The day following, when the people which stood on the other side of the sea saw that there was none other boat there, save that one whereinto his disciples were entered, and that Jesus went not with his disciples into the boat, but that his disciples were gone away alone;
verses.bsb: The next day, the crowd that had remained on the other side of the sea realized that only one boat had been there, and that Jesus had not boarded it with His disciples, but they had gone away alone.

verses.esv: Other boats from Tiberias came near the place where they had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
verses.kjv: (Howbeit there came other boats from Tiberias nigh unto the place where they did eat bread, after that the Lord had given thanks:)
verses.bsb: However, some boats from Tiberias landed near the place where the people had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.

verses.esv: So when the crowd saw that Jesus was not there, nor his disciples, they themselves got into the boats and went to Capernaum, seeking Jesus.
verses.kjv: When the people therefore saw that Jesus was not there, neither his disciples, they also took shipping, and came to Capernaum, seeking for Jesus.
verses.bsb: So when the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor His disciples were there, they got into the boats and went to Capernaum to look for Him.

verses.esv: When they found him on the other side of the sea, they said to him, “Rabbi, when did you come here?”
verses.kjv: And when they had found him on the other side of the sea, they said unto him, Rabbi, when camest thou hither?
verses.bsb: When they found Him on the other side of the sea, they asked Him, “Rabbi, when did You get here?”

verses.esv: Jesus answered them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, you are seeking me, not because you saw signs, but because you ate your fill of the loaves.
verses.kjv: Jesus answered them and said, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Ye seek me, not because ye saw the miracles, but because ye did eat of the loaves, and were filled.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Truly, truly, I tell you, it is not because you saw these signs that you are looking for Me, but because you ate the loaves and had your fill.

verses.esv: Do not work for the food that perishes, but for the food that endures to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give to you. For on him God the Father has set his seal.”
verses.kjv: Labour not for the meat which perisheth, but for that meat which endureth unto everlasting life, which the Son of man shall give unto you: for him hath God the Father sealed.
verses.bsb: Do not work for food that perishes, but for food that endures to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give you. For on Him God the Father has placed His seal of approval.”

verses.esv: Then they said to him, “What must we do, to be doing the works of God?”
verses.kjv: Then said they unto him, What shall we do, that we might work the works of God?
verses.bsb: Then they inquired, “What must we do to perform the works of God?”

verses.esv: Jesus answered them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in him whom he has sent.”
verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto them, This is the work of God, that ye believe on him whom he hath sent.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “The work of God is this: to believe in the One He has sent.”

verses.esv: So they said to him, “Then what sign do you do, that we may see and believe you? What work do you perform?
verses.kjv: They said therefore unto him, What sign shewest thou then, that we may see, and believe thee? what dost thou work?
verses.bsb: So they asked Him, “What sign then will You perform, so that we may see it and believe You? What will You do?

verses.esv: Our fathers ate the manna in the wilderness; as it is written, ‘He gave them bread from heaven to eat.’”
verses.kjv: Our fathers did eat manna in the desert; as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat.
verses.bsb: Our fathers ate the manna in the wilderness, as it is written: ‘He gave them bread from heaven to eat.’”

verses.esv: Jesus then said to them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, it was not Moses who gave you the bread from heaven, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
verses.kjv: Then Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Moses gave you not that bread from heaven; but my Father giveth you the true bread from heaven.
verses.bsb: Jesus said to them, “Truly, truly, I tell you, it was not Moses who gave you the bread from heaven, but it is My Father who gives you the true bread from heaven.

verses.esv: For the bread of God is he who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”
verses.kjv: For the bread of God is he which cometh down from heaven, and giveth life unto the world.
verses.bsb: For the bread of God is He who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”

verses.esv: They said to him, “Sir, give us this bread always.”
verses.kjv: Then said they unto him, Lord, evermore give us this bread.
verses.bsb: “Sir,” they said, “give us this bread at all times.”

verses.esv: Jesus said to them, “I am the bread of life; whoever comes to me shall not hunger, and whoever believes in me shall never thirst.
verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, I am the bread of life: he that cometh to me shall never hunger; and he that believeth on me shall never thirst.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to Me will never hunger, and whoever believes in Me will never thirst.

verses.esv: But I said to you that you have seen me and yet do not believe.
verses.kjv: But I said unto you, That ye also have seen me, and believe not.
verses.bsb: But as I stated, you have seen Me and still you do not believe.

verses.esv: All that the Father gives me will come to me, and whoever comes to me I will never cast out.
verses.kjv: All that the Father giveth me shall come to me; and him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out.
verses.bsb: Everyone the Father gives Me will come to Me, and the one who comes to Me I will never drive away.

verses.esv: For I have come down from heaven, not to do my own will but the will of him who sent me.
verses.kjv: For I came down from heaven, not to do mine own will, but the will of him that sent me.
verses.bsb: For I have come down from heaven, not to do My own will, but to do the will of Him who sent Me.

verses.esv: And this is the will of him who sent me, that I should lose nothing of all that he has given me, but raise it up on the last day.
verses.kjv: And this is the Father’s will which hath sent me, that of all which he hath given me I should lose nothing, but should raise it up again at the last day.
verses.bsb: And this is the will of Him who sent Me, that I shall lose none of those He has given Me, but raise them up at the last day.

verses.esv: For this is the will of my Father, that everyone who looks on the Son and believes in him should have eternal life, and I will raise him up on the last day.”
verses.kjv: And this is the will of him that sent me, that every one which seeth the Son, and believeth on him, may have everlasting life: and I will raise him up at the last day.
verses.bsb: For it is My Father’s will that everyone who looks to the Son and believes in Him shall have eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.”

verses.esv: So the Jews grumbled about him, because he said, “I am the bread that came down from heaven.”
verses.kjv: The Jews then murmured at him, because he said, I am the bread which came down from heaven.
verses.bsb: At this, the Jews began to grumble about Jesus because He had said, “I am the bread that came down from heaven.”

verses.esv: They said, “Is not this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How does he now say, ‘I have come down from heaven’?”
verses.kjv: And they said, Is not this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? how is it then that he saith, I came down from heaven?
verses.bsb: They were asking, “Is this not Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How then can He say, ‘I have come down from heaven?’”

verses.esv: Jesus answered them, “Do not grumble among yourselves.
verses.kjv: Jesus therefore answered and said unto them, Murmur not among yourselves.
verses.bsb: “Stop grumbling among yourselves,” Jesus replied.

verses.esv: No one can come to me unless the Father who sent me draws him. And I will raise him up on the last day.
verses.kjv: No man can come to me, except the Father which hath sent me draw him: and I will raise him up at the last day.
verses.bsb: “No one can come to Me unless the Father who sent Me draws him, and I will raise him up at the last day.

verses.esv: It is written in the Prophets, ‘And they will all be taught by God.’ Everyone who has heard and learned from the Father comes to me—
verses.kjv: It is written in the prophets, And they shall be all taught of God. Every man therefore that hath heard, and hath learned of the Father, cometh unto me.
verses.bsb: It is written in the Prophets: ‘And they will all be taught by God.’ Everyone who has heard the Father and learned from Him comes to Me—

verses.esv: not that anyone has seen the Father except he who is from God; he has seen the Father.
verses.kjv: Not that any man hath seen the Father, save he which is of God, he hath seen the Father.
verses.bsb: not that anyone has seen the Father except the One who is from God; only He has seen the Father.

verses.esv: Truly, truly, I say to you, whoever believes has eternal life.
verses.kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth on me hath everlasting life.
verses.bsb: Truly, truly, I tell you, he who believes has eternal life.

verses.esv: I am the bread of life.
verses.kjv: I am that bread of life.
verses.bsb: I am the bread of life.

verses.esv: Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness, and they died.
verses.kjv: Your fathers did eat manna in the wilderness, and are dead.
verses.bsb: Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness, yet they died.

verses.esv: This is the bread that comes down from heaven, so that one may eat of it and not die.
verses.kjv: This is the bread which cometh down from heaven, that a man may eat thereof, and not die.
verses.bsb: This is the bread that comes down from heaven, so that anyone may eat of it and not die.

verses.esv: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever. And the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.”
verses.kjv: I am the living bread which came down from heaven: if any man eat of this bread, he shall live for ever: and the bread that I will give is my flesh, which I will give for the life of the world.
verses.bsb: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever. And this bread, which I will give for the life of the world, is My flesh.”

verses.esv: The Jews then disputed among themselves, saying, “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”
verses.kjv: The Jews therefore strove among themselves, saying, How can this man give us his flesh to eat?
verses.bsb: At this, the Jews began to argue among themselves, “How can this man give us His flesh to eat?”

verses.esv: So Jesus said to them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
verses.kjv: Then Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except ye eat the flesh of the Son of man, and drink his blood, ye have no life in you.
verses.bsb: So Jesus said to them, “Truly, truly, I tell you, unless you eat the flesh and drink the blood of the Son of Man, you have no life in you.

verses.esv: Whoever feeds on my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up on the last day.
verses.kjv: Whoso eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, hath eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day.
verses.bsb: Whoever eats My flesh and drinks My blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.

verses.esv: For my flesh is true food, and my blood is true drink.
verses.kjv: For my flesh is meat indeed, and my blood is drink indeed.
verses.bsb: For My flesh is real food, and My blood is real drink.

verses.esv: Whoever feeds on my flesh and drinks my blood abides in me, and I in him.
verses.kjv: He that eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, dwelleth in me, and I in him.
verses.bsb: Whoever eats My flesh and drinks My blood remains in Me, and I in him.

verses.esv: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever feeds on me, he also will live because of me.
verses.kjv: As the living Father hath sent me, and I live by the Father: so he that eateth me, even he shall live by me.
verses.bsb: Just as the living Father sent Me and I live because of the Father, so also the one who feeds on Me will live because of Me.

verses.esv: This is the bread that came down from heaven, not like the bread the fathers ate, and died. Whoever feeds on this bread will live forever.”
verses.kjv: This is that bread which came down from heaven: not as your fathers did eat manna, and are dead: he that eateth of this bread shall live for ever.
verses.bsb: This is the bread that came down from heaven. Unlike your fathers, who ate the manna and died, the one who eats this bread will live forever.”

verses.esv: Jesus said these things in the synagogue, as he taught at Capernaum.
verses.kjv: These things said he in the synagogue, as he taught in Capernaum.
verses.bsb: Jesus said this while teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.

verses.esv: When many of his disciples heard it, they said, “This is a hard saying; who can listen to it?”
verses.kjv: Many therefore of his disciples, when they had heard this, said, This is an hard saying; who can hear it?
verses.bsb: On hearing it, many of His disciples said, “This is a difficult teaching. Who can accept it?”

verses.esv: But Jesus, knowing in himself that his disciples were grumbling about this, said to them, “Do you take offense at this?
verses.kjv: When Jesus knew in himself that his disciples murmured at it, he said unto them, Doth this offend you?
verses.bsb: Aware that His disciples were grumbling about this teaching, Jesus asked them, “Does this offend you?

verses.esv: Then what if you were to see the Son of Man ascending to where he was before?
verses.kjv: What and if ye shall see the Son of man ascend up where he was before?
verses.bsb: Then what will happen if you see the Son of Man ascend to where He was before?

verses.esv: It is the Spirit who gives life; the flesh is no help at all. The words that I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
verses.kjv: It is the spirit that quickeneth; the flesh profiteth nothing: the words that I speak unto you, they are spirit, and they are life.
verses.bsb: The Spirit gives life; the flesh profits nothing. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and they are life.

verses.esv: But there are some of you who do not believe.” (For Jesus knew from the beginning who those were who did not believe, and who it was who would betray him.)
verses.kjv: But there are some of you that believe not. For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were that believed not, and who should betray him.
verses.bsb: However, there are some of you who do not believe.” (For Jesus had known from the beginning which of them did not believe and who would betray Him.)

verses.esv: And he said, “This is why I told you that no one can come to me unless it is granted him by the Father.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Therefore said I unto you, that no man can come unto me, except it were given unto him of my Father.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said, “This is why I told you that no one can come to Me unless the Father has granted it to him.”

verses.esv: After this many of his disciples turned back and no longer walked with him.
verses.kjv: ¶ From that time many of his disciples went back, and walked no more with him.
verses.bsb: From that time on many of His disciples turned back and no longer walked with Him.

verses.esv: So Jesus said to the twelve, “Do you want to go away as well?”
verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto the twelve, Will ye also go away?
verses.bsb: So Jesus asked the Twelve, “Do you want to leave too?”

verses.esv: Simon Peter answered him, “Lord, to whom shall we go? You have the words of eternal life,
verses.kjv: Then Simon Peter answered him, Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast the words of eternal life.
verses.bsb: Simon Peter replied, “Lord, to whom would we go? You have the words of eternal life.

verses.esv: and we have believed, and have come to know, that you are the Holy One of God.”
verses.kjv: And we believe and are sure that thou art that Christ, the Son of the living God.
verses.bsb: We believe and know that You are the Holy One of God.”

verses.esv: Jesus answered them, “Did I not choose you, the twelve? And yet one of you is a devil.”
verses.kjv: Jesus answered them, Have not I chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil?
verses.bsb: Jesus answered them, “Have I not chosen you, the Twelve? Yet one of you is a devil!”

verses.esv: He spoke of Judas the son of Simon Iscariot, for he, one of the twelve, was going to betray him.
verses.kjv: He spake of Judas Iscariot the son of Simon: for he it was that should betray him, being one of the twelve.
verses.bsb: He was speaking about Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot. For although Judas was one of the Twelve, he was later to betray Jesus.

verses.esv: After this Jesus went about in Galilee. He would not go about in Judea, because the Jews were seeking to kill him.
verses.kjv: AFTER these things Jesus walked in Galilee: for he would not walk in Jewry, because the Jews sought to kill him.
verses.bsb: After this, Jesus traveled throughout Galilee. He did not want to travel in Judea, because the Jews there were trying to kill Him.

verses.esv: Now the Jews' Feast of Booths was at hand.
verses.kjv: Now the Jews’ feast of tabernacles was at hand.
verses.bsb: However, the Jewish Feast of Tabernacles was near.

verses.esv: So his brothers said to him, “Leave here and go to Judea, that your disciples also may see the works you are doing.
verses.kjv: His brethren therefore said unto him, Depart hence, and go into Judæa, that thy disciples also may see the works that thou doest.
verses.bsb: So Jesus’ brothers said to Him, “Leave here and go to Judea, so that Your disciples there may see the works You are doing.

verses.esv: For no one works in secret if he seeks to be known openly. If you do these things, show yourself to the world.”
verses.kjv: For there is no man that doeth any thing in secret, and he himself seeketh to be known openly. If thou do these things, shew thyself to the world.
verses.bsb: For no one who wants to be known publicly acts in secret. Since You are doing these things, show Yourself to the world.”

verses.esv: For not even his brothers believed in him.
verses.kjv: For neither did his brethren believe in him.
verses.bsb: For even His own brothers did not believe in Him.

verses.esv: Jesus said to them, “My time has not yet come, but your time is always here.
verses.kjv: Then Jesus said unto them, My time is not yet come: but your time is alway ready.
verses.bsb: Therefore Jesus told them, “Although your time is always at hand, My time has not yet come.

verses.esv: The world cannot hate you, but it hates me because I testify about it that its works are evil.
verses.kjv: The world cannot hate you; but me it hateth, because I testify of it, that the works thereof are evil.
verses.bsb: The world cannot hate you, but it hates Me, because I testify that its works are evil.

verses.esv: You go up to the feast. I am not going up to this feast, for my time has not yet fully come.”
verses.kjv: Go ye up unto this feast: I go not up yet unto this feast; for my time is not yet full come.
verses.bsb: Go up to the feast on your own. I am not going up to this feast, because My time has not yet come.”

verses.esv: After saying this, he remained in Galilee.
verses.kjv: When he had said these words unto them, he abode still in Galilee.
verses.bsb: Having said this, Jesus remained in Galilee.

verses.esv: But after his brothers had gone up to the feast, then he also went up, not publicly but in private.
verses.kjv: ¶ But when his brethren were gone up, then went he also up unto the feast, not openly, but as it were in secret.
verses.bsb: But after His brothers had gone up to the feast, He also went—not publicly, but in secret.

verses.esv: The Jews were looking for him at the feast, and saying, “Where is he?”
verses.kjv: Then the Jews sought him at the feast, and said, Where is he?
verses.bsb: So the Jews were looking for Him at the feast and asking, “Where is He?”

verses.esv: And there was much muttering about him among the people. While some said, “He is a good man,” others said, “No, he is leading the people astray.”
verses.kjv: And there was much murmuring among the people concerning him: for some said, He is a good man: others said, Nay; but he deceiveth the people.
verses.bsb: Many in the crowds were whispering about Him. Some said, “He is a good man.”  But others replied, “No, He deceives the people.”

verses.esv: Yet for fear of the Jews no one spoke openly of him.
verses.kjv: Howbeit no man spake openly of him for fear of the Jews.
verses.bsb: Yet no one would speak publicly about Him for fear of the Jews.

verses.esv: About the middle of the feast Jesus went up into the temple and began teaching.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now about the midst of the feast Jesus went up into the temple, and taught.
verses.bsb: About halfway through the feast, Jesus went up to the temple courts and began to teach.

verses.esv: The Jews therefore marveled, saying, “How is it that this man has learning, when he has never studied?”
verses.kjv: And the Jews marvelled, saying, How knoweth this man letters, having never learned?
verses.bsb: The Jews were amazed and asked, “How did this man attain such learning without having studied?”

verses.esv: So Jesus answered them, “My teaching is not mine, but his who sent me.
verses.kjv: Jesus answered them, and said, My doctrine is not mine, but his that sent me.
verses.bsb: “My teaching is not My own,” Jesus replied. “It comes from Him who sent Me.

verses.esv: If anyone's will is to do God's will, he will know whether the teaching is from God or whether I am speaking on my own authority.
verses.kjv: If any man will do his will, he shall know of the doctrine, whether it be of God, or whether I speak of myself.
verses.bsb: If anyone desires to do His will, he will know whether My teaching is from God or whether I speak on My own.

verses.esv: The one who speaks on his own authority seeks his own glory; but the one who seeks the glory of him who sent him is true, and in him there is no falsehood.
verses.kjv: He that speaketh of himself seeketh his own glory: but he that seeketh his glory that sent him, the same is true, and no unrighteousness is in him.
verses.bsb: He who speaks on his own authority seeks his own glory, but He who seeks the glory of the One who sent Him is a man of truth; in Him there is no falsehood.

verses.esv: Has not Moses given you the law? Yet none of you keeps the law. Why do you seek to kill me?”
verses.kjv: Did not Moses give you the law, and yet none of you keepeth the law? Why go ye about to kill me?
verses.bsb: Has not Moses given you the law? Yet not one of you keeps it. Why are you trying to kill Me?”

verses.esv: The crowd answered, “You have a demon! Who is seeking to kill you?”
verses.kjv: The people answered and said, Thou hast a devil: who goeth about to kill thee?
verses.bsb: “You have a demon,” the crowd replied. “Who is trying to kill You?”

verses.esv: Jesus answered them, “I did one work, and you all marvel at it.
verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto them, I have done one work, and ye all marvel.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered them, “I did one miracle, and you are all amazed.

verses.esv: Moses gave you circumcision (not that it is from Moses, but from the fathers), and you circumcise a man on the Sabbath.
verses.kjv: Moses therefore gave unto you circumcision; (not because it is of Moses, but of the fathers;) and ye on the sabbath day circumcise a man.
verses.bsb: But because Moses gave you circumcision, you circumcise a boy on the Sabbath (not that it is from Moses, but from the patriarchs.)

verses.esv: If on the Sabbath a man receives circumcision, so that the law of Moses may not be broken, are you angry with me because on the Sabbath I made a man's whole body well?
verses.kjv: If a man on the sabbath day receive circumcision, that the law of Moses should not be broken; are ye angry at me, because I have made a man every whit whole on the sabbath day?
verses.bsb: If a boy can be circumcised on the Sabbath so that the law of Moses will not be broken, why are you angry with Me for making the whole man well on the Sabbath?

verses.esv: Do not judge by appearances, but judge with right judgment.”
verses.kjv: Judge not according to the appearance, but judge righteous judgment.
verses.bsb: Stop judging by outward appearances, and start judging justly.”

verses.esv: Some of the people of Jerusalem therefore said, “Is not this the man whom they seek to kill?
verses.kjv: Then said some of them of Jerusalem, Is not this he, whom they seek to kill?
verses.bsb: Then some of the people of Jerusalem began to say, “Isn’t this the man they are trying to kill?

verses.esv: And here he is, speaking openly, and they say nothing to him! Can it be that the authorities really know that this is the Christ?
verses.kjv: But, lo, he speaketh boldly, and they say nothing unto him. Do the rulers know indeed that this is the very Christ?
verses.bsb: Yet here He is, speaking publicly, and they are not saying anything to Him. Have the rulers truly recognized that this is the Christ?

verses.esv: But we know where this man comes from, and when the Christ appears, no one will know where he comes from.”
verses.kjv: Howbeit we know this man whence he is: but when Christ cometh, no man knoweth whence he is.
verses.bsb: But we know where this man is from. When the Christ comes, no one will know where He is from.”

verses.esv: So Jesus proclaimed, as he taught in the temple, “You know me, and you know where I come from. But I have not come of my own accord. He who sent me is true, and him you do not know.
verses.kjv: Then cried Jesus in the temple as he taught, saying, Ye both know me, and ye know whence I am: and I am not come of myself, but he that sent me is true, whom ye know not.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus, still teaching in the temple courts, cried out, “You know Me, and you know where I am from. I have not come of My own accord, but He who sent Me is true. You do not know Him,

verses.esv: I know him, for I come from him, and he sent me.”
verses.kjv: But I know him: for I am from him, and he hath sent me.
verses.bsb: but I know Him, because I am from Him and He sent Me.”

verses.esv: So they were seeking to arrest him, but no one laid a hand on him, because his hour had not yet come.
verses.kjv: Then they sought to take him: but no man laid hands on him, because his hour was not yet come.
verses.bsb: So they tried to seize Him, but no one laid a hand on Him, because His hour had not yet come.

verses.esv: Yet many of the people believed in him. They said, “When the Christ appears, will he do more signs than this man has done?”
verses.kjv: And many of the people believed on him, and said, When Christ cometh, will he do more miracles than these which this man hath done?
verses.bsb: Many in the crowd, however, believed in Him and said, “When the Christ comes, will He perform more signs than this man?”

verses.esv: The Pharisees heard the crowd muttering these things about him, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent officers to arrest him.
verses.kjv: ¶ The Pharisees heard that the people murmured such things concerning him; and the Pharisees and the chief priests sent officers to take him.
verses.bsb: When the Pharisees heard the crowd whispering these things about Jesus, they and the chief priests sent officers to arrest Him.

verses.esv: Jesus then said, “I will be with you a little longer, and then I am going to him who sent me.
verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto them, Yet a little while am I with you, and then I go unto him that sent me.
verses.bsb: So Jesus said, “I am with you only a little while longer, and then I am going to the One who sent Me.

verses.esv: You will seek me and you will not find me. Where I am you cannot come.”
verses.kjv: Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come.
verses.bsb: You will look for Me, but you will not find Me; and where I am, you cannot come.”

verses.esv: The Jews said to one another, “Where does this man intend to go that we will not find him? Does he intend to go to the Dispersion among the Greeks and teach the Greeks?
verses.kjv: Then said the Jews among themselves, Whither will he go, that we shall not find him? will he go unto the dispersed among the Gentiles, and teach the Gentiles?
verses.bsb: At this, the Jews said to one another, “Where does He intend to go that we will not find Him? Will He go where the Jews are dispersed among the Greeks, and teach the Greeks?

verses.esv: What does he mean by saying, ‘You will seek me and you will not find me,’ and, ‘Where I am you cannot come’?”
verses.kjv: What manner of saying is this that he said, Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come?
verses.bsb: What does He mean by saying, ‘You will look for Me, but you will not find Me,’ and, ‘Where I am, you cannot come’?”

verses.esv: On the last day of the feast, the great day, Jesus stood up and cried out, “If anyone thirsts, let him come to me and drink.
verses.kjv: In the last day, that great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried, saying, If any man thirst, let him come unto me, and drink.
verses.bsb: On the last and greatest day of the feast, Jesus stood up and called out in a loud voice, “If anyone is thirsty, let him come to Me and drink.

verses.esv: Whoever believes in me, as the Scripture has said, ‘Out of his heart will flow rivers of living water.’”
verses.kjv: He that believeth on me, as the scripture hath said, out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.
verses.bsb: Whoever believes in Me, as the Scripture has said: ‘Streams of living water will flow from within him.’”

verses.esv: Now this he said about the Spirit, whom those who believed in him were to receive, for as yet the Spirit had not been given, because Jesus was not yet glorified.
verses.kjv: (But this spake he of the Spirit, which they that believe on him should receive: for the Holy Ghost was not yet given; because that Jesus was not yet glorified.)
verses.bsb: He was speaking about the Spirit, whom those who believed in Him were later to receive. For the Spirit had not yet been given, because Jesus had not yet been glorified.

verses.esv: When they heard these words, some of the people said, “This really is the Prophet.”
verses.kjv: ¶ Many of the people therefore, when they heard this saying, said, Of a truth this is the Prophet.
verses.bsb: On hearing these words, some of the people said, “This is truly the Prophet.”

verses.esv: Others said, “This is the Christ.” But some said, “Is the Christ to come from Galilee?
verses.kjv: Others said, This is the Christ. But some said, Shall Christ come out of Galilee?
verses.bsb: Others declared, “This is the Christ.”  But still others asked, “How can the Christ come from Galilee?

verses.esv: Has not the Scripture said that the Christ comes from the offspring of David, and comes from Bethlehem, the village where David was?”
verses.kjv: Hath not the scripture said, That Christ cometh of the seed of David, and out of the town of Bethlehem, where David was?
verses.bsb: Doesn’t the Scripture say that the Christ will come from the line of David and from Bethlehem, the village where David lived?”

verses.esv: So there was a division among the people over him.
verses.kjv: So there was a division among the people because of him.
verses.bsb: So there was division in the crowd because of Jesus.

verses.esv: Some of them wanted to arrest him, but no one laid hands on him.
verses.kjv: And some of them would have taken him; but no man laid hands on him.
verses.bsb: Some of them wanted to seize Him, but no one laid a hand on Him.

verses.esv: The officers then came to the chief priests and Pharisees, who said to them, “Why did you not bring him?”
verses.kjv: ¶ Then came the officers to the chief priests and Pharisees; and they said unto them, Why have ye not brought him?
verses.bsb: Then the officers returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked them, “Why didn’t you bring Him in?”

verses.esv: The officers answered, “No one ever spoke like this man!”
verses.kjv: The officers answered, Never man spake like this man.
verses.bsb: “Never has anyone spoken like this man!” the officers answered.

verses.esv: The Pharisees answered them, “Have you also been deceived?
verses.kjv: Then answered them the Pharisees, Are ye also deceived?
verses.bsb: “Have you also been deceived?” replied the Pharisees.

verses.esv: Have any of the authorities or the Pharisees believed in him?
verses.kjv: Have any of the rulers or of the Pharisees believed on him?
verses.bsb: “Have any of the rulers or Pharisees believed in Him?

verses.esv: But this crowd that does not know the law is accursed.”
verses.kjv: But this people who knoweth not the law are cursed.
verses.bsb: But this crowd that does not know the law, they are under a curse.”

verses.esv: Nicodemus, who had gone to him before, and who was one of them, said to them,
verses.kjv: Nicodemus saith unto them, (he that came to Jesus by night, being one of them,)
verses.bsb: Nicodemus, who had gone to Jesus earlier and who himself was one of them, asked,

verses.esv: “Does our law judge a man without first giving him a hearing and learning what he does?”
verses.kjv: Doth our law judge any man, before it hear him, and know what he doeth?
verses.bsb: “Does our law convict a man without first hearing from him to determine what he has done?”

verses.esv: They replied, “Are you from Galilee too? Search and see that no prophet arises from Galilee.” [The earliest manuscripts do not include 7:53–8:11.] [[They went each to his own house, but Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
verses.kjv: They answered and said unto him, Art thou also of Galilee? Search, and look: for out of Galilee ariseth no prophet.
verses.bsb: “Aren’t you also from Galilee?” they replied. “Look into it, and you will see that no prophet comes out of Galilee.”

verses.esv: Early in the morning he came again to the temple. All the people came to him, and he sat down and taught them.
verses.kjv: And early in the morning he came again into the temple, and all the people came unto him; and he sat down, and taught them.
verses.bsb: Early in the morning He went back into the temple courts. All the people came to Him, and He sat down to teach them.

verses.esv: The scribes and the Pharisees brought a woman who had been caught in adultery, and placing her in the midst
verses.kjv: And the scribes and Pharisees brought unto him a woman taken in adultery; and when they had set her in the midst,
verses.bsb: The scribes and Pharisees, however, brought to Him a woman caught in adultery. They made her stand before them

verses.esv: they said to him, “Teacher, this woman has been caught in the act of adultery.
verses.kjv: They say unto him, Master, this woman was taken in adultery, in the very act.
verses.bsb: and said, “Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of adultery.

verses.esv: Now in the Law, Moses commanded us to stone such women. So what do you say?”
verses.kjv: Now Moses in the law commanded us, that such should be stoned: but what sayest thou?
verses.bsb: In the Law Moses commanded us to stone such a woman. So what do You say?”

verses.esv: This they said to test him, that they might have some charge to bring against him. Jesus bent down and wrote with his finger on the ground.
verses.kjv: This they said, tempting him, that they might have to accuse him. But Jesus stooped down, and with his finger wrote on the ground, as though he heard them not.
verses.bsb: They said this to test Him, in order to have a basis for accusing Him. But Jesus bent down and began to write on the ground with His finger.

verses.esv: And as they continued to ask him, he stood up and said to them, “Let him who is without sin among you be the first to throw a stone at her.”
verses.kjv: So when they continued asking him, he lifted up himself, and said unto them, He that is without sin among you, let him first cast a stone at her.
verses.bsb: When they continued to question Him, He straightened up and said to them, “Let him who is without sin among you be the first to cast a stone at her.”

verses.esv: And once more he bent down and wrote on the ground.
verses.kjv: And again he stooped down, and wrote on the ground.
verses.bsb: And again He bent down and wrote on the ground.

verses.esv: But when they heard it, they went away one by one, beginning with the older ones, and Jesus was left alone with the woman standing before him.
verses.kjv: And they which heard it, being convicted by their own conscience, went out one by one, beginning at the eldest, even unto the last: and Jesus was left alone, and the woman standing in the midst.
verses.bsb: When they heard this, they began to go away one by one, beginning with the older ones, until only Jesus was left, with the woman standing there.

verses.esv: Jesus stood up and said to her, “Woman, where are they? Has no one condemned you?”
verses.kjv: When Jesus had lifted up himself, and saw none but the woman, he said unto her, Woman, where are those thine accusers? hath no man condemned thee?
verses.bsb: Then Jesus straightened up and asked her, “Woman, where are your accusers? Has no one condemned you?”

verses.esv: She said, “No one, Lord.” And Jesus said, “Neither do I condemn you; go, and from now on sin no more.”]]
verses.kjv: She said, No man, Lord. And Jesus said unto her, Neither do I condemn thee: go, and sin no more.
verses.bsb: “No one, Lord,” she answered.  “Then neither do I condemn you,” Jesus declared. “Now go and sin no more.”

verses.esv: Again Jesus spoke to them, saying, “I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will not walk in darkness, but will have the light of life.”
verses.kjv: ¶ Then spake Jesus again unto them, saying, I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall not walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life.
verses.bsb: Once again, Jesus spoke to the people and said, “I am the light of the world. Whoever follows Me will never walk in the darkness, but will have the light of life.”

verses.esv: So the Pharisees said to him, “You are bearing witness about yourself; your testimony is not true.”
verses.kjv: The Pharisees therefore said unto him, Thou bearest record of thyself; thy record is not true.
verses.bsb: So the Pharisees said to Him, “You are testifying about Yourself; Your testimony is not valid.”

verses.esv: Jesus answered, “Even if I do bear witness about myself, my testimony is true, for I know where I came from and where I am going, but you do not know where I come from or where I am going.
verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto them, Though I bear record of myself, yet my record is true: for I know whence I came, and whither I go; but ye cannot tell whence I come, and whither I go.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Even if I testify about Myself, My testimony is valid, because I know where I came from and where I am going. But you do not know where I came from or where I am going.

verses.esv: You judge according to the flesh; I judge no one.
verses.kjv: Ye judge after the flesh; I judge no man.
verses.bsb: You judge according to the flesh; I judge no one.

verses.esv: Yet even if I do judge, my judgment is true, for it is not I alone who judge, but I and the Father who sent me.
verses.kjv: And yet if I judge, my judgment is true: for I am not alone, but I and the Father that sent me.
verses.bsb: But even if I do judge, My judgment is true, because I am not alone; I am with the Father who sent Me.

verses.esv: In your Law it is written that the testimony of two people is true.
verses.kjv: It is also written in your law, that the testimony of two men is true.
verses.bsb: Even in your own Law it is written that the testimony of two men is valid.

verses.esv: I am the one who bears witness about myself, and the Father who sent me bears witness about me.”
verses.kjv: I am one that bear witness of myself, and the Father that sent me beareth witness of me.
verses.bsb: I am One who testifies about Myself, and the Father, who sent Me, also testifies about Me.”

verses.esv: They said to him therefore, “Where is your Father?” Jesus answered, “You know neither me nor my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also.”
verses.kjv: Then said they unto him, Where is thy Father? Jesus answered, Ye neither know me, nor my Father: if ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also.
verses.bsb: “Where is Your Father?” they asked Him.  “You do not know Me or My Father,” Jesus answered. “If you knew Me, you would know My Father as well.”

verses.esv: These words he spoke in the treasury, as he taught in the temple; but no one arrested him, because his hour had not yet come.
verses.kjv: These words spake Jesus in the treasury, as he taught in the temple: and no man laid hands on him; for his hour was not yet come.
verses.bsb: He spoke these words while teaching in the temple courts, near the treasury. Yet no one seized Him, because His hour had not yet come.

verses.esv: So he said to them again, “I am going away, and you will seek me, and you will die in your sin. Where I am going, you cannot come.”
verses.kjv: Then said Jesus again unto them, I go my way, and ye shall seek me, and shall die in your sins: whither I go, ye cannot come.
verses.bsb: Again He said to them, “I am going away, and you will look for Me, but you will die in your sin. Where I am going, you cannot come.”

verses.esv: So the Jews said, “Will he kill himself, since he says, ‘Where I am going, you cannot come’?”
verses.kjv: Then said the Jews, Will he kill himself? because he saith, Whither I go, ye cannot come.
verses.bsb: So the Jews began to ask, “Will He kill Himself, since He says, ‘Where I am going, you cannot come’?”

verses.esv: He said to them, “You are from below; I am from above. You are of this world; I am not of this world.
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Ye are from beneath; I am from above: ye are of this world; I am not of this world.
verses.bsb: Then He told them, “You are from below; I am from above. You are of this world; I am not of this world.

verses.esv: I told you that you would die in your sins, for unless you believe that I am he you will die in your sins.”
verses.kjv: I said therefore unto you, that ye shall die in your sins: for if ye believe not that I am he, ye shall die in your sins.
verses.bsb: That is why I told you that you would die in your sins. For unless you believe that I am He, you will die in your sins.”

verses.esv: So they said to him, “Who are you?” Jesus said to them, “Just what I have been telling you from the beginning.
verses.kjv: Then said they unto him, Who art thou? And Jesus saith unto them, Even the same that I said unto you from the beginning.
verses.bsb: “Who are You?” they asked.  “Just what I have been telling you from the beginning,” Jesus replied.

verses.esv: I have much to say about you and much to judge, but he who sent me is true, and I declare to the world what I have heard from him.”
verses.kjv: I have many things to say and to judge of you: but he that sent me is true; and I speak to the world those things which I have heard of him.
verses.bsb: “I have much to say about you and much to judge. But the One who sent Me is truthful, and what I have heard from Him, I tell the world.”

verses.esv: They did not understand that he had been speaking to them about the Father.
verses.kjv: They understood not that he spake to them of the Father.
verses.bsb: They did not understand that He was telling them about the Father.

verses.esv: So Jesus said to them, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am he, and that I do nothing on my own authority, but speak just as the Father taught me.
verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto them, When ye have lifted up the Son of man, then shall ye know that I am he, and that I do nothing of myself; but as my Father hath taught me, I speak these things.
verses.bsb: So Jesus said, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am He, and that I do nothing on My own, but speak exactly what the Father has taught Me.

verses.esv: And he who sent me is with me. He has not left me alone, for I always do the things that are pleasing to him.”
verses.kjv: And he that sent me is with me: the Father hath not left me alone; for I do always those things that please him.
verses.bsb: He who sent Me is with Me. He has not left Me alone, because I always do what pleases Him.”

verses.esv: As he was saying these things, many believed in him.
verses.kjv: As he spake these words, many believed on him.
verses.bsb: As Jesus spoke these things, many believed in Him.

verses.esv: So Jesus said to the Jews who had believed him, “If you abide in my word, you are truly my disciples,
verses.kjv: Then said Jesus to those Jews which believed on him, If ye continue in my word, then are ye my disciples indeed;
verses.bsb: So He said to the Jews who had believed Him, “If you continue in My word, you are truly My disciples.

verses.esv: and you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”
verses.kjv: And ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.
verses.bsb: Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”

verses.esv: They answered him, “We are offspring of Abraham and have never been enslaved to anyone. How is it that you say, ‘You will become free’?”
verses.kjv: ¶ They answered him, We be Abraham’s seed, and were never in bondage to any man: how sayest thou, Ye shall be made free?
verses.bsb: “We are Abraham’s descendants,” they answered. “We have never been slaves to anyone. How can You say we will be set free?”

verses.esv: Jesus answered them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, everyone who practices sin is a slave to sin.
verses.kjv: Jesus answered them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whosoever committeth sin is the servant of sin.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Truly, truly, I tell you, everyone who sins is a slave to sin.

verses.esv: The slave does not remain in the house forever; the son remains forever.
verses.kjv: And the servant abideth not in the house for ever: but the Son abideth ever.
verses.bsb: A slave is not a permanent member of the family, but a son belongs to it forever.

verses.esv: So if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.
verses.kjv: If the Son therefore shall make you free, ye shall be free indeed.
verses.bsb: So if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.

verses.esv: I know that you are offspring of Abraham; yet you seek to kill me because my word finds no place in you.
verses.kjv: I know that ye are Abraham’s seed; but ye seek to kill me, because my word hath no place in you.
verses.bsb: I know you are Abraham’s descendants, but you are trying to kill Me because My word has no place within you.

verses.esv: I speak of what I have seen with my Father, and you do what you have heard from your father.”
verses.kjv: I speak that which I have seen with my Father: and ye do that which ye have seen with your father.
verses.bsb: I speak of what I have seen in the presence of the Father, and you do what you have heard from your father.”

verses.esv: They answered him, “Abraham is our father.” Jesus said to them, “If you were Abraham's children, you would be doing the works Abraham did,
verses.kjv: They answered and said unto him, Abraham is our father. Jesus saith unto them, If ye were Abraham’s children, ye would do the works of Abraham.
verses.bsb: “Abraham is our father,” they replied.  “If you were children of Abraham,” said Jesus, “you would do the works of Abraham.

verses.esv: but now you seek to kill me, a man who has told you the truth that I heard from God. This is not what Abraham did.
verses.kjv: But now ye seek to kill me, a man that hath told you the truth, which I have heard of God: this did not Abraham.
verses.bsb: But now you are trying to kill Me, a man who has told you the truth that I heard from God. Abraham never did such a thing.

verses.esv: You are doing the works your father did.” They said to him, “We were not born of sexual immorality. We have one Father—even God.”
verses.kjv: Ye do the deeds of your father. Then said they to him, We be not born of fornication; we have one Father, even God.
verses.bsb: You are doing the works of your father.”  “We are not illegitimate children,” they declared. “Our only Father is God Himself.”

verses.esv: Jesus said to them, “If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God and I am here. I came not of my own accord, but he sent me.
verses.kjv: Jesus said unto them, If God were your Father, ye would love me: for I proceeded forth and came from God; neither came I of myself, but he sent me.
verses.bsb: Jesus said to them, “If God were your Father, you would love Me, for I have come here from God. I have not come on My own, but He sent Me.

verses.esv: Why do you not understand what I say? It is because you cannot bear to hear my word.
verses.kjv: Why do ye not understand my speech? even because ye cannot hear my word.
verses.bsb: Why do you not understand what I am saying? It is because you are unable to accept My message.

verses.esv: You are of your father the devil, and your will is to do your father's desires. He was a murderer from the beginning, and does not stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks out of his own character, for he is a liar and the father of lies.
verses.kjv: Ye are of your father the devil, and the lusts of your father ye will do. He was a murderer from the beginning, and abode not in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaketh a lie, he speaketh of his own: for he is a liar, and the father of it.
verses.bsb: You belong to your father, the devil, and you want to carry out his desires. He was a murderer from the beginning, refusing to uphold the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks his native language, because he is a liar and the father of lies.

verses.esv: But because I tell the truth, you do not believe me.
verses.kjv: And because I tell you the truth, ye believe me not.
verses.bsb: But because I speak the truth, you do not believe Me!

verses.esv: Which one of you convicts me of sin? If I tell the truth, why do you not believe me?
verses.kjv: Which of you convinceth me of sin? And if I say the truth, why do ye not believe me?
verses.bsb: Which of you can prove Me guilty of sin? If I speak the truth, why do you not believe Me?

verses.esv: Whoever is of God hears the words of God. The reason why you do not hear them is that you are not of God.”
verses.kjv: He that is of God heareth God’s words: ye therefore hear them not, because ye are not of God.
verses.bsb: Whoever belongs to God hears the words of God. The reason you do not hear is that you do not belong to God.”

verses.esv: The Jews answered him, “Are we not right in saying that you are a Samaritan and have a demon?”
verses.kjv: Then answered the Jews, and said unto him, Say we not well that thou art a Samaritan, and hast a devil?
verses.bsb: The Jews answered Him, “Are we not right to say that You are a Samaritan and You have a demon?”

verses.esv: Jesus answered, “I do not have a demon, but I honor my Father, and you dishonor me.
verses.kjv: Jesus answered, I have not a devil; but I honour my Father, and ye do dishonour me.
verses.bsb: “I do not have a demon,” Jesus replied, “but I honor My Father, and you dishonor Me.

verses.esv: Yet I do not seek my own glory; there is One who seeks it, and he is the judge.
verses.kjv: And I seek not mine own glory: there is one that seeketh and judgeth.
verses.bsb: I do not seek My own glory. There is One who seeks it, and He is the Judge.

verses.esv: Truly, truly, I say to you, if anyone keeps my word, he will never see death.”
verses.kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto you, If a man keep my saying, he shall never see death.
verses.bsb: Truly, truly, I tell you, if anyone keeps My word, he will never see death.”

verses.esv: The Jews said to him, “Now we know that you have a demon! Abraham died, as did the prophets, yet you say, ‘If anyone keeps my word, he will never taste death.’
verses.kjv: Then said the Jews unto him, Now we know that thou hast a devil. Abraham is dead, and the prophets; and thou sayest, If a man keep my saying, he shall never taste of death.
verses.bsb: “Now we know that You have a demon!” declared the Jews. “Abraham died, and so did the prophets, yet You say that anyone who keeps Your word will never taste death.

verses.esv: Are you greater than our father Abraham, who died? And the prophets died! Who do you make yourself out to be?”
verses.kjv: Art thou greater than our father Abraham, which is dead? and the prophets are dead: whom makest thou thyself?
verses.bsb: Are You greater than our father Abraham? He died, as did the prophets. Who do You claim to be?”

verses.esv: Jesus answered, “If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing. It is my Father who glorifies me, of whom you say, ‘He is our God.’
verses.kjv: Jesus answered, If I honour myself, my honour is nothing: it is my Father that honoureth me; of whom ye say, that he is your God:
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “If I glorify Myself, My glory means nothing. The One who glorifies Me is My Father, of whom you say ‘He is our God.’

verses.esv: But you have not known him. I know him. If I were to say that I do not know him, I would be a liar like you, but I do know him and I keep his word.
verses.kjv: Yet ye have not known him; but I know him: and if I should say, I know him not, I shall be a liar like unto you: but I know him, and keep his saying.
verses.bsb: You do not know Him, but I know Him. If I said I did not know Him, I would be a liar like you. But I do know Him, and I keep His word.

verses.esv: Your father Abraham rejoiced that he would see my day. He saw it and was glad.”
verses.kjv: Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day: and he saw it, and was glad.
verses.bsb: Your father Abraham rejoiced that he would see My day. He saw it and was glad.”

verses.esv: So the Jews said to him, “You are not yet fifty years old, and have you seen Abraham?”
verses.kjv: Then said the Jews unto him, Thou art not yet fifty years old, and hast thou seen Abraham?
verses.bsb: Then the Jews said to Him, “You are not yet fifty years old, and You have seen Abraham?”

verses.esv: Jesus said to them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, before Abraham was, I am.”
verses.kjv: Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Before Abraham was, I am.
verses.bsb: “Truly, truly, I tell you,” Jesus declared, “before Abraham was born, I am!”

verses.esv: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and went out of the temple.
verses.kjv: Then took they up stones to cast at him: but Jesus hid himself, and went out of the temple, going through the midst of them, and so passed by.
verses.bsb: At this, they picked up stones to throw at Him. But Jesus was hidden and went out of the temple area.

verses.esv: As he passed by, he saw a man blind from birth.
verses.kjv: AND as Jesus passed by, he saw a man which was blind from his birth.
verses.bsb: Now as Jesus was passing by, He saw a man blind from birth,

verses.esv: And his disciples asked him, “Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?”
verses.kjv: And his disciples asked him, saying, Master, who did sin, this man, or his parents, that he was born blind?
verses.bsb: and His disciples asked Him, “Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?”

verses.esv: Jesus answered, “It was not that this man sinned, or his parents, but that the works of God might be displayed in him.
verses.kjv: Jesus answered, Neither hath this man sinned, nor his parents: but that the works of God should be made manifest in him.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “Neither this man nor his parents sinned, but this happened so that the works of God would be displayed in him.

verses.esv: We must work the works of him who sent me while it is day; night is coming, when no one can work.
verses.kjv: I must work the works of him that sent me, while it is day: the night cometh, when no man can work.
verses.bsb: While it is daytime, we must do the works of Him who sent Me. Night is coming, when no one can work.

verses.esv: As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.”
verses.kjv: As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
verses.bsb: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.”

verses.esv: Having said these things, he spit on the ground and made mud with the saliva. Then he anointed the man's eyes with the mud
verses.kjv: When he had thus spoken, he spat on the ground, and made clay of the spittle, and he anointed the eyes of the blind man with the clay,
verses.bsb: When Jesus had said this, He spit on the ground, made some mud, and applied it to the man’s eyes.

verses.esv: and said to him, “Go, wash in the pool of Siloam” (which means Sent). So he went and washed and came back seeing.
verses.kjv: And said unto him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam, (which is by interpretation, Sent.) He went his way therefore, and washed, and came seeing.
verses.bsb: Then He told him, “Go, wash in the Pool of Siloam” (which means “Sent”). So the man went and washed, and came back seeing.

verses.esv: The neighbors and those who had seen him before as a beggar were saying, “Is this not the man who used to sit and beg?”
verses.kjv: ¶ The neighbours therefore, and they which before had seen him that he was blind, said, Is not this he that sat and begged?
verses.bsb: At this, his neighbors and those who had formerly seen him begging began to ask, “Isn’t this the man who used to sit and beg?”

verses.esv: Some said, “It is he.” Others said, “No, but he is like him.” He kept saying, “I am the man.”
verses.kjv: Some said, This is he: others said, He is like him: but he said, I am he.
verses.bsb: Some claimed that he was, but others said, “No, he just looks like him.”  But the man kept saying, “I am the one.”

verses.esv: So they said to him, “Then how were your eyes opened?”
verses.kjv: Therefore said they unto him, How were thine eyes opened?
verses.bsb: “How then were your eyes opened?” they asked.

verses.esv: He answered, “The man called Jesus made mud and anointed my eyes and said to me, ‘Go to Siloam and wash.’ So I went and washed and received my sight.”
verses.kjv: He answered and said, A man that is called Jesus made clay, and anointed mine eyes, and said unto me, Go to the pool of Siloam, and wash: and I went and washed, and I received sight.
verses.bsb: He answered, “The man they call Jesus made some mud and anointed my eyes, and He told me to go to Siloam and wash. So I went and washed and received my sight.”

verses.esv: They said to him, “Where is he?” He said, “I do not know.”
verses.kjv: Then said they unto him, Where is he? He said, I know not.
verses.bsb: “Where is He?” they asked.  “I do not know,” he answered.

verses.esv: They brought to the Pharisees the man who had formerly been blind.
verses.kjv: ¶ They brought to the Pharisees him that aforetime was blind.
verses.bsb: They brought to the Pharisees the man who had been blind.

verses.esv: Now it was a Sabbath day when Jesus made the mud and opened his eyes.
verses.kjv: And it was the sabbath day when Jesus made the clay, and opened his eyes.
verses.bsb: Now the day on which Jesus had made the mud and opened his eyes was a Sabbath.

verses.esv: So the Pharisees again asked him how he had received his sight. And he said to them, “He put mud on my eyes, and I washed, and I see.”
verses.kjv: Then again the Pharisees also asked him how he had received his sight. He said unto them, He put clay upon mine eyes, and I washed, and do see.
verses.bsb: So the Pharisees also asked him how he had received his sight.  The man answered, “He put mud on my eyes, and I washed, and now I can see.”

verses.esv: Some of the Pharisees said, “This man is not from God, for he does not keep the Sabbath.” But others said, “How can a man who is a sinner do such signs?” And there was a division among them.
verses.kjv: Therefore said some of the Pharisees, This man is not of God, because he keepeth not the sabbath day. Others said, How can a man that is a sinner do such miracles? And there was a division among them.
verses.bsb: Because of this, some of the Pharisees said, “This man is not from God, for He does not keep the Sabbath.”  But others said, “How can a sinful man perform such signs?”  And there was division among them.

verses.esv: So they said again to the blind man, “What do you say about him, since he has opened your eyes?” He said, “He is a prophet.”
verses.kjv: They say unto the blind man again, What sayest thou of him, that he hath opened thine eyes? He said, He is a prophet.
verses.bsb: So once again they asked the man who had been blind, “What do you say about Him, since it was your eyes He opened?”  “He is a prophet,” the man replied.

verses.esv: The Jews did not believe that he had been blind and had received his sight, until they called the parents of the man who had received his sight
verses.kjv: But the Jews did not believe concerning him, that he had been blind, and received his sight, until they called the parents of him that had received his sight.
verses.bsb: The Jews still did not believe that the man had been blind and had received his sight until they summoned his parents

verses.esv: and asked them, “Is this your son, who you say was born blind? How then does he now see?”
verses.kjv: And they asked them, saying, Is this your son, who ye say was born blind? how then doth he now see?
verses.bsb: and asked, “Is this your son, the one you say was born blind? So how is it that he can now see?”

verses.esv: His parents answered, “We know that this is our son and that he was born blind.
verses.kjv: His parents answered them and said, We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind:
verses.bsb: His parents answered, “We know he is our son, and we know he was born blind.

verses.esv: But how he now sees we do not know, nor do we know who opened his eyes. Ask him; he is of age. He will speak for himself.”
verses.kjv: But by what means he now seeth, we know not; or who hath opened his eyes, we know not: he is of age; ask him: he shall speak for himself.
verses.bsb: But how he can now see or who opened his eyes, we do not know. Ask him. He is old enough to speak for himself.”

verses.esv: (His parents said these things because they feared the Jews, for the Jews had already agreed that if anyone should confess Jesus to be Christ, he was to be put out of the synagogue.)
verses.kjv: These words spake his parents, because they feared the Jews: for the Jews had agreed already, that if any man did confess that he was Christ, he should be put out of the synagogue.
verses.bsb: His parents said this because they were afraid of the Jews. For the Jews had already determined that anyone who confessed Jesus as the Christ would be put out of the synagogue.

verses.esv: Therefore his parents said, “He is of age; ask him.”
verses.kjv: Therefore said his parents, He is of age; ask him.
verses.bsb: That was why his parents said, “He is old enough. Ask him.”

verses.esv: So for the second time they called the man who had been blind and said to him, “Give glory to God. We know that this man is a sinner.”
verses.kjv: Then again called they the man that was blind, and said unto him, Give God the praise: we know that this man is a sinner.
verses.bsb: So a second time they called for the man who had been blind and said, “Give glory to God! We know that this man is a sinner.”

verses.esv: He answered, “Whether he is a sinner I do not know. One thing I do know, that though I was blind, now I see.”
verses.kjv: He answered and said, Whether he be a sinner or no, I know not: one thing I know, that, whereas I was blind, now I see.
verses.bsb: He answered, “Whether He is a sinner I do not know. There is one thing I do know: I was blind, but now I see!”

verses.esv: They said to him, “What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?”
verses.kjv: Then said they to him again, What did he to thee? how opened he thine eyes?
verses.bsb: “What did He do to you?” they asked. “How did He open your eyes?”

verses.esv: He answered them, “I have told you already, and you would not listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you also want to become his disciples?”
verses.kjv: He answered them, I have told you already, and ye did not hear: wherefore would ye hear it again? will ye also be his disciples?
verses.bsb: He replied, “I already told you, and you did not listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you also want to become His disciples?”

verses.esv: And they reviled him, saying, “You are his disciple, but we are disciples of Moses.
verses.kjv: Then they reviled him, and said, Thou art his disciple; but we are Moses’ disciples.
verses.bsb: Then they heaped insults on him and said, “You are His disciple; we are disciples of Moses.

verses.esv: We know that God has spoken to Moses, but as for this man, we do not know where he comes from.”
verses.kjv: We know that God spake unto Moses: as for this fellow, we know not from whence he is.
verses.bsb: We know that God spoke to Moses, but we do not know where this man is from.”

verses.esv: The man answered, “Why, this is an amazing thing! You do not know where he comes from, and yet he opened my eyes.
verses.kjv: The man answered and said unto them, Why herein is a marvellous thing, that ye know not from whence he is, and yet he hath opened mine eyes.
verses.bsb: “That is remarkable indeed!” the man said. “You do not know where He is from, and yet He opened my eyes.

verses.esv: We know that God does not listen to sinners, but if anyone is a worshiper of God and does his will, God listens to him.
verses.kjv: Now we know that God heareth not sinners: but if any man be a worshipper of God, and doeth his will, him he heareth.
verses.bsb: We know that God does not listen to sinners, but He does listen to the one who worships Him and does His will.

verses.esv: Never since the world began has it been heard that anyone opened the eyes of a man born blind.
verses.kjv: Since the world began was it not heard that any man opened the eyes of one that was born blind.
verses.bsb: Never before has anyone heard of opening the eyes of a man born blind.

verses.esv: If this man were not from God, he could do nothing.”
verses.kjv: If this man were not of God, he could do nothing.
verses.bsb: If this man were not from God, He could do no such thing.”

verses.esv: They answered him, “You were born in utter sin, and would you teach us?” And they cast him out.
verses.kjv: They answered and said unto him, Thou wast altogether born in sins, and dost thou teach us? And they cast him out.
verses.bsb: They replied, “You were born in utter sin, and you are instructing us?” And they threw him out.

verses.esv: Jesus heard that they had cast him out, and having found him he said, “Do you believe in the Son of Man?”
verses.kjv: Jesus heard that they had cast him out; and when he had found him, he said unto him, Dost thou believe on the Son of God?
verses.bsb: When Jesus heard that they had thrown him out, He found the man and said, “Do you believe in the Son of Man?”

verses.esv: He answered, “And who is he, sir, that I may believe in him?”
verses.kjv: He answered and said, Who is he, Lord, that I might believe on him?
verses.bsb: “Who is He, Sir?” he replied. “Tell me so that I may believe in Him.”

verses.esv: Jesus said to him, “You have seen him, and it is he who is speaking to you.”
verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Thou hast both seen him, and it is he that talketh with thee.
verses.bsb: “You have already seen Him,” Jesus answered. “He is the One speaking with you.”

verses.esv: He said, “Lord, I believe,” and he worshiped him.
verses.kjv: And he said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped him.
verses.bsb: “Lord, I believe,” he said. And he worshiped Jesus.

verses.esv: Jesus said, “For judgment I came into this world, that those who do not see may see, and those who see may become blind.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jesus said, For judgment I am come into this world, that they which see not might see; and that they which see might be made blind.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus declared, “For judgment I have come into this world, so that the blind may see and those who see may become blind.”

verses.esv: Some of the Pharisees near him heard these things, and said to him, “Are we also blind?”
verses.kjv: And some of the Pharisees which were with him heard these words, and said unto him, Are we blind also?
verses.bsb: Some of the Pharisees who were with Him heard this, and they asked Him, “Are we blind too?”

verses.esv: Jesus said to them, “If you were blind, you would have no guilt; but now that you say, ‘We see,’ your guilt remains.
verses.kjv: Jesus said unto them, If ye were blind, ye should have no sin: but now ye say, We see; therefore your sin remaineth.
verses.bsb: “If you were blind,” Jesus replied, “you would not be guilty of sin. But since you claim you can see, your guilt remains.”

verses.esv: “Truly, truly, I say to you, he who does not enter the sheepfold by the door but climbs in by another way, that man is a thief and a robber.
verses.kjv: VERILY, verily, I say unto you, He that entereth not by the door into the sheepfold, but climbeth up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber.
verses.bsb: “Truly, truly, I tell you, whoever does not enter the sheepfold by the gate, but climbs in some other way, is a thief and a robber.

verses.esv: But he who enters by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.
verses.kjv: But he that entereth in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.
verses.bsb: But the one who enters by the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.

verses.esv: To him the gatekeeper opens. The sheep hear his voice, and he calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
verses.kjv: To him the porter openeth; and the sheep hear his voice: and he calleth his own sheep by name, and leadeth them out.
verses.bsb: The gatekeeper opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen for his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.

verses.esv: When he has brought out all his own, he goes before them, and the sheep follow him, for they know his voice.
verses.kjv: And when he putteth forth his own sheep, he goeth before them, and the sheep follow him: for they know his voice.
verses.bsb: When he has brought out all his own, he goes on ahead of them, and his sheep follow him because they know his voice.

verses.esv: A stranger they will not follow, but they will flee from him, for they do not know the voice of strangers.”
verses.kjv: And a stranger will they not follow, but will flee from him: for they know not the voice of strangers.
verses.bsb: But they will never follow a stranger; in fact, they will flee from him because they do not recognize his voice.”

verses.esv: This figure of speech Jesus used with them, but they did not understand what he was saying to them.
verses.kjv: This parable spake Jesus unto them: but they understood not what things they were which he spake unto them.
verses.bsb: Jesus spoke to them using this illustration, but they did not understand what He was telling them.

verses.esv: So Jesus again said to them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, I am the door of the sheep.
verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto them again, Verily, verily, I say unto you, I am the door of the sheep.
verses.bsb: So He said to them again, “Truly, truly, I tell you, I am the gate for the sheep.

verses.esv: All who came before me are thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not listen to them.
verses.kjv: All that ever came before me are thieves and robbers: but the sheep did not hear them.
verses.bsb: All who came before Me were thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not listen to them.

verses.esv: I am the door. If anyone enters by me, he will be saved and will go in and out and find pasture.
verses.kjv: I am the door: by me if any man enter in, he shall be saved, and shall go in and out, and find pasture.
verses.bsb: I am the gate. If anyone enters through Me, he will be saved. He will come in and go out and find pasture.

verses.esv: The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy. I came that they may have life and have it abundantly.
verses.kjv: The thief cometh not, but for to steal, and to kill, and to destroy: I am come that they might have life, and that they might have it more abundantly.
verses.bsb: The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy. I have come that they may have life, and have it in all its fullness.

verses.esv: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
verses.kjv: I am the good shepherd: the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep.
verses.bsb: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down His life for the sheep.

verses.esv: He who is a hired hand and not a shepherd, who does not own the sheep, sees the wolf coming and leaves the sheep and flees, and the wolf snatches them and scatters them.
verses.kjv: But he that is an hireling, and not the shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, seeth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep, and fleeth: and the wolf catcheth them, and scattereth the sheep.
verses.bsb: The hired hand is not the shepherd, and the sheep are not his own. When he sees the wolf coming, he abandons the sheep and runs away. Then the wolf pounces on them and scatters the flock.

verses.esv: He flees because he is a hired hand and cares nothing for the sheep.
verses.kjv: The hireling fleeth, because he is an hireling, and careth not for the sheep.
verses.bsb: The man runs away because he is a hired servant and is unconcerned for the sheep.

verses.esv: I am the good shepherd. I know my own and my own know me,
verses.kjv: I am the good shepherd, and know my sheep, and am known of mine.
verses.bsb: I am the good shepherd. I know My sheep and My sheep know Me,

verses.esv: just as the Father knows me and I know the Father; and I lay down my life for the sheep.
verses.kjv: As the Father knoweth me, even so know I the Father: and I lay down my life for the sheep.
verses.bsb: just as the Father knows Me and I know the Father. And I lay down My life for the sheep.

verses.esv: And I have other sheep that are not of this fold. I must bring them also, and they will listen to my voice. So there will be one flock, one shepherd.
verses.kjv: And other sheep I have, which are not of this fold: them also I must bring, and they shall hear my voice; and there shall be one fold, and one shepherd.
verses.bsb: I have other sheep that are not of this fold. I must bring them in as well, and they will listen to My voice. Then there will be one flock and one shepherd.

verses.esv: For this reason the Father loves me, because I lay down my life that I may take it up again.
verses.kjv: Therefore doth my Father love me, because I lay down my life, that I might take it again.
verses.bsb: The reason the Father loves Me is that I lay down My life in order to take it up again.

verses.esv: No one takes it from me, but I lay it down of my own accord. I have authority to lay it down, and I have authority to take it up again. This charge I have received from my Father.”
verses.kjv: No man taketh it from me, but I lay it down of myself. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again. This commandment have I received of my Father.
verses.bsb: No one takes it from Me, but I lay it down of My own accord. I have authority to lay it down and authority to take it up again. This charge I have received from My Father.”

verses.esv: There was again a division among the Jews because of these words.
verses.kjv: ¶ There was a division therefore again among the Jews for these sayings.
verses.bsb: Again there was division among the Jews because of Jesus’ message.

verses.esv: Many of them said, “He has a demon, and is insane; why listen to him?”
verses.kjv: And many of them said, He hath a devil, and is mad; why hear ye him?
verses.bsb: Many of them said, “He is demon-possessed and insane. Why would you listen to Him?”

verses.esv: Others said, “These are not the words of one who is oppressed by a demon. Can a demon open the eyes of the blind?”
verses.kjv: Others said, These are not the words of him that hath a devil. Can a devil open the eyes of the blind?
verses.bsb: But others replied, “These are not the words of a man possessed by a demon. Can a demon open the eyes of the blind?”

verses.esv: At that time the Feast of Dedication took place at Jerusalem. It was winter,
verses.kjv: ¶ And it was at Jerusalem the feast of the dedication, and it was winter.
verses.bsb: At that time the Feast of Dedication took place in Jerusalem. It was winter,

verses.esv: and Jesus was walking in the temple, in the colonnade of Solomon.
verses.kjv: And Jesus walked in the temple in Solomon’s porch.
verses.bsb: and Jesus was walking in the temple courts in Solomon’s Colonnade.

verses.esv: So the Jews gathered around him and said to him, “How long will you keep us in suspense? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.”
verses.kjv: Then came the Jews round about him, and said unto him, How long dost thou make us to doubt? If thou be the Christ, tell us plainly.
verses.bsb: So the Jews gathered around Him and demanded, “How long will You keep us in suspense? If You are the Christ, tell us plainly.”

verses.esv: Jesus answered them, “I told you, and you do not believe. The works that I do in my Father's name bear witness about me,
verses.kjv: Jesus answered them, I told you, and ye believed not: the works that I do in my Father’s name, they bear witness of me.
verses.bsb: “I already told you,” Jesus replied, “but you did not believe. The works I do in My Father’s name testify on My behalf.

verses.esv: but you do not believe because you are not among my sheep.
