verses.kjv: But ye believe not, because ye are not of my sheep, as I said unto you.
verses.bsb: But because you are not My sheep, you refuse to believe.

verses.esv: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me.
verses.kjv: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me:
verses.bsb: My sheep listen to My voice; I know them, and they follow Me.

verses.esv: I give them eternal life, and they will never perish, and no one will snatch them out of my hand.
verses.kjv: And I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish, neither shall any man pluck them out of my hand.
verses.bsb: I give them eternal life, and they will never perish. No one can snatch them out of My hand.

verses.esv: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all, and no one is able to snatch them out of the Father's hand.
verses.kjv: My Father, which gave them me, is greater than all; and no man is able to pluck them out of my Father’s hand.
verses.bsb: My Father who has given them to Me is greater than all. No one can snatch them out of My Father’s hand.

verses.esv: I and the Father are one.”
verses.kjv: I and my Father are one.
verses.bsb: I and the Father are one.”

verses.esv: The Jews picked up stones again to stone him.
verses.kjv: Then the Jews took up stones again to stone him.
verses.bsb: At this, the Jews again picked up stones to stone Him.

verses.esv: Jesus answered them, “I have shown you many good works from the Father; for which of them are you going to stone me?”
verses.kjv: Jesus answered them, Many good works have I shewed you from my Father; for which of those works do ye stone me?
verses.bsb: But Jesus responded, “I have shown you many good works from the Father. For which of these do you stone Me?”

verses.esv: The Jews answered him, “It is not for a good work that we are going to stone you but for blasphemy, because you, being a man, make yourself God.”
verses.kjv: The Jews answered him, saying, For a good work we stone thee not; but for blasphemy; and because that thou, being a man, makest thyself God.
verses.bsb: “We are not stoning You for any good work,” said the Jews, “but for blasphemy, because You, who are a man, declare Yourself to be God.”

verses.esv: Jesus answered them, “Is it not written in your Law, ‘I said, you are gods’?
verses.kjv: Jesus answered them, Is it not written in your law, I said, Ye are gods?
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Is it not written in your Law: ‘I have said you are gods’?

verses.esv: If he called them gods to whom the word of God came—and Scripture cannot be broken—
verses.kjv: If he called them gods, unto whom the word of God came, and the scripture cannot be broken;
verses.bsb: If he called them gods to whom the word of God came—and the Scripture cannot be broken—

verses.esv: do you say of him whom the Father consecrated and sent into the world, ‘You are blaspheming,’ because I said, ‘I am the Son of God’?
verses.kjv: Say ye of him, whom the Father hath sanctified, and sent into the world, Thou blasphemest; because I said, I am the Son of God?
verses.bsb: then what about the One whom the Father sanctified and sent into the world? How then can you accuse Me of blasphemy for stating that I am the Son of God?

verses.esv: If I am not doing the works of my Father, then do not believe me;
verses.kjv: If I do not the works of my Father, believe me not.
verses.bsb: If I am not doing the works of My Father, then do not believe Me.

verses.esv: but if I do them, even though you do not believe me, believe the works, that you may know and understand that the Father is in me and I am in the Father.”
verses.kjv: But if I do, though ye believe not me, believe the works: that ye may know, and believe, that the Father is in me, and I in him.
verses.bsb: But if I am doing them, even though you do not believe Me, believe the works themselves, so that you may know and understand that the Father is in Me, and I am in the Father.”

verses.esv: Again they sought to arrest him, but he escaped from their hands.
verses.kjv: Therefore they sought again to take him: but he escaped out of their hand,
verses.bsb: At this, they tried again to seize Him, but He escaped their grasp.

verses.esv: He went away again across the Jordan to the place where John had been baptizing at first, and there he remained.
verses.kjv: And went away again beyond Jordan into the place where John at first baptized; and there he abode.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus went back across the Jordan to the place where John had first been baptizing, and He stayed there.

verses.esv: And many came to him. And they said, “John did no sign, but everything that John said about this man was true.”
verses.kjv: And many resorted unto him, and said, John did no miracle: but all things that John spake of this man were true.
verses.bsb: Many came to Him and said, “Although John never performed a sign, everything he said about this man was true.”

verses.esv: And many believed in him there.
verses.kjv: And many believed on him there.
verses.bsb: And many in that place believed in Jesus.

verses.esv: Now a certain man was ill, Lazarus of Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.
verses.kjv: NOW a certain man was sick, named Lazarus, of Bethany, the town of Mary and her sister Martha.
verses.bsb: At this time a man named Lazarus was sick. He lived in Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.

verses.esv: It was Mary who anointed the Lord with ointment and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was ill.
verses.kjv: (It was that Mary which anointed the Lord with ointment, and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was sick.)
verses.bsb: (Mary, whose brother Lazarus was sick, was to anoint the Lord with perfume and wipe His feet with her hair.)

verses.esv: So the sisters sent to him, saying, “Lord, he whom you love is ill.”
verses.kjv: Therefore his sisters sent unto him, saying, Lord, behold, he whom thou lovest is sick.
verses.bsb: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, “Lord, the one You love is sick.”

verses.esv: But when Jesus heard it he said, “This illness does not lead to death. It is for the glory of God, so that the Son of God may be glorified through it.”
verses.kjv: When Jesus heard that, he said, This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God might be glorified thereby.
verses.bsb: When Jesus heard this, He said, “This sickness will not end in death. No, it is for the glory of God, so that the Son of God may be glorified through it.”

verses.esv: Now Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus.
verses.kjv: Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.
verses.bsb: Now Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus.

verses.esv: So, when he heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed two days longer in the place where he was.
verses.kjv: When he had heard therefore that he was sick, he abode two days still in the same place where he was.
verses.bsb: So on hearing that Lazarus was sick, He stayed where He was for two days,

verses.esv: Then after this he said to the disciples, “Let us go to Judea again.”
verses.kjv: Then after that saith he to his disciples, Let us go into Judæa again.
verses.bsb: and then He said to the disciples, “Let us go back to Judea.”

verses.esv: The disciples said to him, “Rabbi, the Jews were just now seeking to stone you, and are you going there again?”
verses.kjv: His disciples say unto him, Master, the Jews of late sought to stone thee; and goest thou thither again?
verses.bsb: “Rabbi,” they replied, “the Jews just tried to stone You, and You are going back there?”

verses.esv: Jesus answered, “Are there not twelve hours in the day? If anyone walks in the day, he does not stumble, because he sees the light of this world.
verses.kjv: Jesus answered, Are there not twelve hours in the day? If any man walk in the day, he stumbleth not, because he seeth the light of this world.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “Are there not twelve hours of daylight? If anyone walks in the daytime, he will not stumble, because he sees by the light of this world.

verses.esv: But if anyone walks in the night, he stumbles, because the light is not in him.”
verses.kjv: But if a man walk in the night, he stumbleth, because there is no light in him.
verses.bsb: But if anyone walks at night, he will stumble, because he has no light.”

verses.esv: After saying these things, he said to them, “Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep, but I go to awaken him.”
verses.kjv: These things said he: and after that he saith unto them, Our friend Lazarus sleepeth; but I go, that I may awake him out of sleep.
verses.bsb: After He had said this, He told them, “Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep, but I am going there to wake him up.”

verses.esv: The disciples said to him, “Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will recover.”
verses.kjv: Then said his disciples, Lord, if he sleep, he shall do well.
verses.bsb: His disciples replied, “Lord, if he is sleeping, he will get better.”

verses.esv: Now Jesus had spoken of his death, but they thought that he meant taking rest in sleep.
verses.kjv: Howbeit Jesus spake of his death: but they thought that he had spoken of taking of rest in sleep.
verses.bsb: They thought that Jesus was talking about actual sleep, but He was speaking about the death of Lazarus.

verses.esv: Then Jesus told them plainly, “Lazarus has died,
verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto them plainly, Lazarus is dead.
verses.bsb: So Jesus told them plainly, “Lazarus is dead,

verses.esv: and for your sake I am glad that I was not there, so that you may believe. But let us go to him.”
verses.kjv: And I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, to the intent ye may believe; nevertheless let us go unto him.
verses.bsb: and for your sake I am glad I was not there, so that you may believe. But let us go to him.”

verses.esv: So Thomas, called the Twin, said to his fellow disciples, “Let us also go, that we may die with him.”
verses.kjv: Then said Thomas, which is called Didymus, unto his fellowdisciples, Let us also go, that we may die with him.
verses.bsb: Then Thomas called Didymus said to his fellow disciples, “Let us also go, so that we may die with Him.”

verses.esv: Now when Jesus came, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days.
verses.kjv: Then when Jesus came, he found that he had lain in the grave four days already.
verses.bsb: When Jesus arrived, He found that Lazarus had already spent four days in the tomb.

verses.esv: Bethany was near Jerusalem, about two miles off,
verses.kjv: Now Bethany was nigh unto Jerusalem, about fifteen furlongs off:
verses.bsb: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, a little less than two miles away,

verses.esv: and many of the Jews had come to Martha and Mary to console them concerning their brother.
verses.kjv: And many of the Jews came to Martha and Mary, to comfort them concerning their brother.
verses.bsb: and many of the Jews had come to Martha and Mary to console them in the loss of their brother.

verses.esv: So when Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went and met him, but Mary remained seated in the house.
verses.kjv: Then Martha, as soon as she heard that Jesus was coming, went and met him: but Mary sat still in the house.
verses.bsb: So when Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went out to meet Him; but Mary stayed at home.

verses.esv: Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.
verses.kjv: Then said Martha unto Jesus, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.
verses.bsb: Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if You had been here, my brother would not have died.

verses.esv: But even now I know that whatever you ask from God, God will give you.”
verses.kjv: But I know, that even now, whatsoever thou wilt ask of God, God will give it thee.
verses.bsb: But even now I know that God will give You whatever You ask of Him.”

verses.esv: Jesus said to her, “Your brother will rise again.”
verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, Thy brother shall rise again.
verses.bsb: “Your brother will rise again,” Jesus told her.

verses.esv: Martha said to him, “I know that he will rise again in the resurrection on the last day.”
verses.kjv: Martha saith unto him, I know that he shall rise again in the resurrection at the last day.
verses.bsb: Martha replied, “I know that he will rise again in the resurrection at the last day.”

verses.esv: Jesus said to her, “I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me, though he die, yet shall he live,
verses.kjv: Jesus said unto her, I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live:
verses.bsb: Jesus said to her, “I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in Me will live, even though he dies.

verses.esv: and everyone who lives and believes in me shall never die. Do you believe this?”
verses.kjv: And whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die. Believest thou this?
verses.bsb: And everyone who lives and believes in Me will never die. Do you believe this?”

verses.esv: She said to him, “Yes, Lord; I believe that you are the Christ, the Son of God, who is coming into the world.”
verses.kjv: She saith unto him, Yea, Lord: I believe that thou art the Christ, the Son of God, which should come into the world.
verses.bsb: “Yes, Lord,” she answered, “I believe that You are the Christ, the Son of God, who was to come into the world.”

verses.esv: When she had said this, she went and called her sister Mary, saying in private, “The Teacher is here and is calling for you.”
verses.kjv: And when she had so said, she went her way, and called Mary her sister secretly, saying, The Master is come, and calleth for thee.
verses.bsb: After Martha had said this, she went back and called her sister Mary aside to tell her, “The Teacher is here and is asking for you.”

verses.esv: And when she heard it, she rose quickly and went to him.
verses.kjv: As soon as she heard that, she arose quickly, and came unto him.
verses.bsb: And when Mary heard this, she got up quickly and went to Him.

verses.esv: Now Jesus had not yet come into the village, but was still in the place where Martha had met him.
verses.kjv: Now Jesus was not yet come into the town, but was in that place where Martha met him.
verses.bsb: Now Jesus had not yet entered the village, but was still at the place where Martha had met Him.

verses.esv: When the Jews who were with her in the house, consoling her, saw Mary rise quickly and go out, they followed her, supposing that she was going to the tomb to weep there.
verses.kjv: The Jews then which were with her in the house, and comforted her, when they saw Mary, that she rose up hastily and went out, followed her, saying, She goeth unto the grave to weep there.
verses.bsb: When the Jews who were in the house consoling Mary saw how quickly she got up and went out, they followed her, supposing she was going to the tomb to mourn there.

verses.esv: Now when Mary came to where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet, saying to him, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.”
verses.kjv: Then when Mary was come where Jesus was, and saw him, she fell down at his feet, saying unto him, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.
verses.bsb: When Mary came to Jesus and saw Him, she fell at His feet and said, “Lord, if You had been here, my brother would not have died.”

verses.esv: When Jesus saw her weeping, and the Jews who had come with her also weeping, he was deeply moved in his spirit and greatly troubled.
verses.kjv: When Jesus therefore saw her weeping, and the Jews also weeping which came with her, he groaned in the spirit, and was troubled,
verses.bsb: When Jesus saw her weeping, and the Jews who had come with her also weeping, He was deeply moved in spirit and troubled.

verses.esv: And he said, “Where have you laid him?” They said to him, “Lord, come and see.”
verses.kjv: And said, Where have ye laid him? They said unto him, Lord, come and see.
verses.bsb: “Where have you put him?” He asked.  “Come and see, Lord,” they answered.

verses.esv: Jesus wept.
verses.kjv: Jesus wept.
verses.bsb: Jesus wept.

verses.esv: So the Jews said, “See how he loved him!”
verses.kjv: Then said the Jews, Behold how he loved him!
verses.bsb: Then the Jews said, “See how He loved him!”

verses.esv: But some of them said, “Could not he who opened the eyes of the blind man also have kept this man from dying?”
verses.kjv: And some of them said, Could not this man, which opened the eyes of the blind, have caused that even this man should not have died?
verses.bsb: But some of them asked, “Could not this man who opened the eyes of the blind also have kept Lazarus from dying?”

verses.esv: Then Jesus, deeply moved again, came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone lay against it.
verses.kjv: Jesus therefore again groaning in himself cometh to the grave. It was a cave, and a stone lay upon it.
verses.bsb: Jesus, once again deeply moved, came to the tomb. It was a cave with a stone laid across the entrance.

verses.esv: Jesus said, “Take away the stone.” Martha, the sister of the dead man, said to him, “Lord, by this time there will be an odor, for he has been dead four days.”
verses.kjv: Jesus said, Take ye away the stone. Martha, the sister of him that was dead, saith unto him, Lord, by this time he stinketh: for he hath been dead four days.
verses.bsb: “Take away the stone,” Jesus said.  “Lord, by now he stinks,” said Martha, the sister of the dead man. “It has already been four days.”

verses.esv: Jesus said to her, “Did I not tell you that if you believed you would see the glory of God?”
verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, Said I not unto thee, that, if thou wouldest believe, thou shouldest see the glory of God?
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Did I not tell you that if you believed, you would see the glory of God?”

verses.esv: So they took away the stone. And Jesus lifted up his eyes and said, “Father, I thank you that you have heard me.
verses.kjv: Then they took away the stone from the place where the dead was laid. And Jesus lifted up his eyes, and said, Father, I thank thee that thou hast heard me.
verses.bsb: So they took away the stone. Then Jesus lifted His eyes upward and said, “Father, I thank You that You have heard Me.

verses.esv: I knew that you always hear me, but I said this on account of the people standing around, that they may believe that you sent me.”
verses.kjv: And I knew that thou hearest me always: but because of the people which stand by I said it, that they may believe that thou hast sent me.
verses.bsb: I knew that You always hear Me, but I say this for the benefit of the people standing here, so they may believe that You sent Me.”

verses.esv: When he had said these things, he cried out with a loud voice, “Lazarus, come out.”
verses.kjv: And when he thus had spoken, he cried with a loud voice, Lazarus, come forth.
verses.bsb: After Jesus had said this, He called out in a loud voice, “Lazarus, come out!”

verses.esv: The man who had died came out, his hands and feet bound with linen strips, and his face wrapped with a cloth. Jesus said to them, “Unbind him, and let him go.”
verses.kjv: And he that was dead came forth, bound hand and foot with graveclothes: and his face was bound about with a napkin. Jesus saith unto them, Loose him, and let him go.
verses.bsb: The man who had been dead came out with his hands and feet bound in strips of linen, and his face wrapped in a cloth.  “Unwrap him and let him go,” Jesus told them.

verses.esv: Many of the Jews therefore, who had come with Mary and had seen what he did, believed in him,
verses.kjv: Then many of the Jews which came to Mary, and had seen the things which Jesus did, believed on him.
verses.bsb: Therefore many of the Jews who had come to Mary, and had seen what Jesus did, believed in Him.

verses.esv: but some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
verses.kjv: But some of them went their ways to the Pharisees, and told them what things Jesus had done.
verses.bsb: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.

verses.esv: So the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered the council and said, “What are we to do? For this man performs many signs.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then gathered the chief priests and the Pharisees a council, and said, What do we? for this man doeth many miracles.
verses.bsb: Then the chief priests and Pharisees convened the Sanhedrin and said, “What are we to do? This man is performing many signs.

verses.esv: If we let him go on like this, everyone will believe in him, and the Romans will come and take away both our place and our nation.”
verses.kjv: If we let him thus alone, all men will believe on him: and the Romans shall come and take away both our place and nation.
verses.bsb: If we let Him go on like this, everyone will believe in Him, and then the Romans will come and take away both our place and our nation.”

verses.esv: But one of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, said to them, “You know nothing at all.
verses.kjv: And one of them, named Caiaphas, being the high priest that same year, said unto them, Ye know nothing at all,
verses.bsb: But one of them, named Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, said to them, “You know nothing at all!

verses.esv: Nor do you understand that it is better for you that one man should die for the people, not that the whole nation should perish.”
verses.kjv: Nor consider that it is expedient for us, that one man should die for the people, and that the whole nation perish not.
verses.bsb: You do not realize that it is better for you that one man die for the people than that the whole nation perish.”

verses.esv: He did not say this of his own accord, but being high priest that year he prophesied that Jesus would die for the nation,
verses.kjv: And this spake he not of himself: but being high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus should die for that nation;
verses.bsb: Caiaphas did not say this on his own. Instead, as high priest that year, he was prophesying that Jesus would die for the nation,

verses.esv: and not for the nation only, but also to gather into one the children of God who are scattered abroad.
verses.kjv: And not for that nation only, but that also he should gather together in one the children of God that were scattered abroad.
verses.bsb: and not only for the nation, but also for the scattered children of God, to gather them together into one.

verses.esv: So from that day on they made plans to put him to death.
verses.kjv: Then from that day forth they took counsel together for to put him to death.
verses.bsb: So from that day on they plotted to kill Him.

verses.esv: Jesus therefore no longer walked openly among the Jews, but went from there to the region near the wilderness, to a town called Ephraim, and there he stayed with the disciples.
verses.kjv: Jesus therefore walked no more openly among the Jews; but went thence unto a country near to the wilderness, into a city called Ephraim, and there continued with his disciples.
verses.bsb: As a result, Jesus no longer went about publicly among the Jews, but He withdrew to a town called Ephraim in an area near the wilderness. And He stayed there with the disciples.

verses.esv: Now the Passover of the Jews was at hand, and many went up from the country to Jerusalem before the Passover to purify themselves.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Jews’ passover was nigh at hand: and many went out of the country up to Jerusalem before the passover, to purify themselves.
verses.bsb: Now the Jewish Passover was near, and many people went up from the country to Jerusalem to purify themselves before the Passover.

verses.esv: They were looking for Jesus and saying to one another as they stood in the temple, “What do you think? That he will not come to the feast at all?”
verses.kjv: Then sought they for Jesus, and spake among themselves, as they stood in the temple, What think ye, that he will not come to the feast?
verses.bsb: They kept looking for Jesus and asking one another as they stood in the temple courts, “What do you think? Will He come to the feast at all?”

verses.esv: Now the chief priests and the Pharisees had given orders that if anyone knew where he was, he should let them know, so that they might arrest him.
verses.kjv: Now both the chief priests and the Pharisees had given a commandment, that, if any man knew where he were, he should shew it, that they might take him.
verses.bsb: But the chief priests and Pharisees had given orders that anyone who knew where He was must report it, so that they could arrest Him.

verses.esv: Six days before the Passover, Jesus therefore came to Bethany, where Lazarus was, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
verses.kjv: THEN Jesus six days before the passover came to Bethany, where Lazarus was which had been dead, whom he raised from the dead.
verses.bsb: Six days before the Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, the hometown of Lazarus, whom He had raised from the dead.

verses.esv: So they gave a dinner for him there. Martha served, and Lazarus was one of those reclining with him at table.
verses.kjv: There they made him a supper; and Martha served: but Lazarus was one of them that sat at the table with him.
verses.bsb: So they hosted a dinner for Jesus there. Martha served, and Lazarus was among those reclining at the table with Him.

verses.esv: Mary therefore took a pound of expensive ointment made from pure nard, and anointed the feet of Jesus and wiped his feet with her hair. The house was filled with the fragrance of the perfume.
verses.kjv: Then took Mary a pound of ointment of spikenard, very costly, and anointed the feet of Jesus, and wiped his feet with her hair: and the house was filled with the odour of the ointment.
verses.bsb: Then Mary took about a pint of expensive perfume, made of pure nard, and she anointed Jesus’ feet and wiped them with her hair. And the house was filled with the fragrance of the perfume.

verses.esv: But Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples (he who was about to betray him), said,
verses.kjv: Then saith one of his disciples, Judas Iscariot, Simon’s son, which should betray him,
verses.bsb: But one of His disciples, Judas Iscariot, who was going to betray Him, asked,

verses.esv: “Why was this ointment not sold for three hundred denarii and given to the poor?”
verses.kjv: Why was not this ointment sold for three hundred pence, and given to the poor?
verses.bsb: “Why wasn’t this perfume sold for three hundred denarii and the money given to the poor?”

verses.esv: He said this, not because he cared about the poor, but because he was a thief, and having charge of the moneybag he used to help himself to what was put into it.
verses.kjv: This he said, not that he cared for the poor; but because he was a thief, and had the bag, and bare what was put therein.
verses.bsb: Judas did not say this because he cared about the poor, but because he was a thief. As keeper of the money bag, he used to take from what was put into it.

verses.esv: Jesus said, “Leave her alone, so that she may keep it for the day of my burial.
verses.kjv: Then said Jesus, Let her alone: against the day of my burying hath she kept this.
verses.bsb: “Leave her alone,” Jesus replied. “She has kept this perfume in preparation for the day of My burial.

verses.esv: For the poor you always have with you, but you do not always have me.”
verses.kjv: For the poor always ye have with you; but me ye have not always.
verses.bsb: The poor you will always have with you, but you will not always have Me.”

verses.esv: When the large crowd of the Jews learned that Jesus was there, they came, not only on account of him but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
verses.kjv: Much people of the Jews therefore knew that he was there: and they came not for Jesus’ sake only, but that they might see Lazarus also, whom he had raised from the dead.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile a large crowd of Jews learned that Jesus was there. And they came not only because of Him, but also to see Lazarus, whom He had raised from the dead.

verses.esv: So the chief priests made plans to put Lazarus to death as well,
verses.kjv: ¶ But the chief priests consulted that they might put Lazarus also to death;
verses.bsb: So the chief priests made plans to kill Lazarus as well,

verses.esv: because on account of him many of the Jews were going away and believing in Jesus.
verses.kjv: Because that by reason of him many of the Jews went away, and believed on Jesus.
verses.bsb: for on account of him many of the Jews were deserting them and believing in Jesus.

verses.esv: The next day the large crowd that had come to the feast heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: ¶ On the next day much people that were come to the feast, when they heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem,
verses.bsb: The next day the great crowd that had come to the feast heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.

verses.esv: So they took branches of palm trees and went out to meet him, crying out, “Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord, even the King of Israel!”
verses.kjv: Took branches of palm trees, and went forth to meet him, and cried, Hosanna: Blessed is the King of Israel that cometh in the name of the Lord.
verses.bsb: They took palm branches and went out to meet Him, shouting:  “Hosanna!”   “Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord!”   “Blessed is the King of Israel!”

verses.esv: And Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it, just as it is written,
verses.kjv: And Jesus, when he had found a young ass, sat thereon; as it is written,
verses.bsb: Finding a young donkey, Jesus sat on it, as it is written:

verses.esv: “Fear not, daughter of Zion; behold, your king is coming, sitting on a donkey's colt!”
verses.kjv: Fear not, daughter of Sion: behold, thy King cometh, sitting on an ass’s colt.
verses.bsb: “Do not be afraid, O Daughter of Zion. See, your King is coming, seated on the colt of a donkey.”

verses.esv: His disciples did not understand these things at first, but when Jesus was glorified, then they remembered that these things had been written about him and had been done to him.
verses.kjv: These things understood not his disciples at the first: but when Jesus was glorified, then remembered they that these things were written of him, and that they had done these things unto him.
verses.bsb: At first His disciples did not understand these things, but after Jesus was glorified they remembered what had been done to Him, and they realized that these very things had also been written about Him.

verses.esv: The crowd that had been with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead continued to bear witness.
verses.kjv: The people therefore that was with him when he called Lazarus out of his grave, and raised him from the dead, bare record.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, many people continued to testify that they had been with Jesus when He called Lazarus from the tomb and raised him from the dead.

verses.esv: The reason why the crowd went to meet him was that they heard he had done this sign.
verses.kjv: For this cause the people also met him, for that they heard that he had done this miracle.
verses.bsb: That is also why the crowd went out to meet Him, because they heard that He had performed this sign.

verses.esv: So the Pharisees said to one another, “You see that you are gaining nothing. Look, the world has gone after him.”
verses.kjv: The Pharisees therefore said among themselves, Perceive ye how ye prevail nothing? behold, the world is gone after him.
verses.bsb: Then the Pharisees said to one another, “You can see that this is doing you no good. Look how the whole world has gone after Him!”

verses.esv: Now among those who went up to worship at the feast were some Greeks.
verses.kjv: ¶ And there were certain Greeks among them that came up to worship at the feast:
verses.bsb: Now there were some Greeks among those who went up to worship at the feast.

verses.esv: So these came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and asked him, “Sir, we wish to see Jesus.”
verses.kjv: The same came therefore to Philip, which was of Bethsaida of Galilee, and desired him, saying, Sir, we would see Jesus.
verses.bsb: They came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and requested of him, “Sir, we want to see Jesus.”

verses.esv: Philip went and told Andrew; Andrew and Philip went and told Jesus.
verses.kjv: Philip cometh and telleth Andrew: and again Andrew and Philip tell Jesus.
verses.bsb: Philip relayed this appeal to Andrew, and both of them went and told Jesus.

verses.esv: And Jesus answered them, “The hour has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jesus answered them, saying, The hour is come, that the Son of man should be glorified.
verses.bsb: But Jesus replied, “The hour has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.

verses.esv: Truly, truly, I say to you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains alone; but if it dies, it bears much fruit.
verses.kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except a corn of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone: but if it die, it bringeth forth much fruit.
verses.bsb: Truly, truly, I tell you, unless a kernel of wheat falls to the ground and dies, it remains only a seed; but if it dies, it bears much fruit.

verses.esv: Whoever loves his life loses it, and whoever hates his life in this world will keep it for eternal life.
verses.kjv: He that loveth his life shall lose it; and he that hateth his life in this world shall keep it unto life eternal.
verses.bsb: Whoever loves his life will lose it, but whoever hates his life in this world will keep it for eternal life.

verses.esv: If anyone serves me, he must follow me; and where I am, there will my servant be also. If anyone serves me, the Father will honor him.
verses.kjv: If any man serve me, let him follow me; and where I am, there shall also my servant be: if any man serve me, him will my Father honour.
verses.bsb: If anyone serves Me, he must follow Me; and where I am, My servant will be as well. If anyone serves Me, the Father will honor him.

verses.esv: “Now is my soul troubled. And what shall I say? ‘Father, save me from this hour’? But for this purpose I have come to this hour.
verses.kjv: Now is my soul troubled; and what shall I say? Father, save me from this hour: but for this cause came I unto this hour.
verses.bsb: Now My soul is troubled, and what shall I say? ‘Father, save Me from this hour’? No, it is for this purpose that I have come to this hour.

verses.esv: Father, glorify your name.” Then a voice came from heaven: “I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.”
verses.kjv: Father, glorify thy name. Then came there a voice from heaven, saying, I have both glorified it, and will glorify it again.
verses.bsb: Father, glorify Your name!”  Then a voice came from heaven: “I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.”

verses.esv: The crowd that stood there and heard it said that it had thundered. Others said, “An angel has spoken to him.”
verses.kjv: The people therefore, that stood by, and heard it, said that it thundered: others said, An angel spake to him.
verses.bsb: The crowd standing there heard it and said that it had thundered. Others said that an angel had spoken to Him.

verses.esv: Jesus answered, “This voice has come for your sake, not mine.
verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said, This voice came not because of me, but for your sakes.
verses.bsb: In response, Jesus said, “This voice was not for My benefit, but yours.

verses.esv: Now is the judgment of this world; now will the ruler of this world be cast out.
verses.kjv: Now is the judgment of this world: now shall the prince of this world be cast out.
verses.bsb: Now judgment is upon this world; now the prince of this world will be cast out.

verses.esv: And I, when I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all people to myself.”
verses.kjv: And I, if I be lifted up from the earth, will draw all men unto me.
verses.bsb: And I, when I am lifted up from the earth, will draw everyone to Myself.”

verses.esv: He said this to show by what kind of death he was going to die.
verses.kjv: This he said, signifying what death he should die.
verses.bsb: He said this to indicate the kind of death He was going to die.

verses.esv: So the crowd answered him, “We have heard from the Law that the Christ remains forever. How can you say that the Son of Man must be lifted up? Who is this Son of Man?”
verses.kjv: The people answered him, We have heard out of the law that Christ abideth for ever: and how sayest thou, The Son of man must be lifted up? who is this Son of man?
verses.bsb: The crowd replied, “We have heard from the Law that the Christ will remain forever. So how can You say that the Son of Man must be lifted up? Who is this Son of Man?”

verses.esv: So Jesus said to them, “The light is among you for a little while longer. Walk while you have the light, lest darkness overtake you. The one who walks in the darkness does not know where he is going.
verses.kjv: Then Jesus said unto them, Yet a little while is the light with you. Walk while ye have the light, lest darkness come upon you: for he that walketh in darkness knoweth not whither he goeth.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus told them, “For a little while longer, the Light will be among you. Walk while you have the Light, so that darkness will not overtake you. The one who walks in the darkness does not know where he is going.

verses.esv: While you have the light, believe in the light, that you may become sons of light.” When Jesus had said these things, he departed and hid himself from them.
verses.kjv: While ye have light, believe in the light, that ye may be the children of light. These things spake Jesus, and departed, and did hide himself from them.
verses.bsb: While you have the Light, believe in the Light, so that you may become sons of light.”  After Jesus had spoken these things, He went away and was hidden from them.

verses.esv: Though he had done so many signs before them, they still did not believe in him,
verses.kjv: ¶ But though he had done so many miracles before them, yet they believed not on him:
verses.bsb: Although Jesus had performed so many signs in their presence, they still did not believe in Him.

verses.esv: so that the word spoken by the prophet Isaiah might be fulfilled: “Lord, who has believed what he heard from us, and to whom has the arm of the Lord been revealed?”
verses.kjv: That the saying of Esaias the prophet might be fulfilled, which he spake, Lord, who hath believed our report? and to whom hath the arm of the Lord been revealed?
verses.bsb: This was to fulfill the word of Isaiah the prophet:  “Lord, who has believed our message? And to whom has the arm of the Lord been revealed?”

verses.esv: Therefore they could not believe. For again Isaiah said,
verses.kjv: Therefore they could not believe, because that Esaias said again,
verses.bsb: For this reason they were unable to believe. For again, Isaiah says:

verses.esv: “He has blinded their eyes and hardened their heart, lest they see with their eyes, and understand with their heart, and turn, and I would heal them.”
verses.kjv: He hath blinded their eyes, and hardened their heart; that they should not see with their eyes, nor understand with their heart, and be converted, and I should heal them.
verses.bsb: “He has blinded their eyes and hardened their hearts, so that they cannot see with their eyes, and understand with their hearts, and turn, and I would heal them.”

verses.esv: Isaiah said these things because he saw his glory and spoke of him.
verses.kjv: These things said Esaias, when he saw his glory, and spake of him.
verses.bsb: Isaiah said these things because he saw Jesus’ glory and spoke about Him.

verses.esv: Nevertheless, many even of the authorities believed in him, but for fear of the Pharisees they did not confess it, so that they would not be put out of the synagogue;
verses.kjv: ¶ Nevertheless among the chief rulers also many believed on him; but because of the Pharisees they did not confess him, lest they should be put out of the synagogue:
verses.bsb: Nevertheless, many of the leaders believed in Him; but because of the Pharisees they did not confess Him, for fear that they would be put out of the synagogue.

verses.esv: for they loved the glory that comes from man more than the glory that comes from God.
verses.kjv: For they loved the praise of men more than the praise of God.
verses.bsb: For they loved praise from men more than praise from God.

verses.esv: And Jesus cried out and said, “Whoever believes in me, believes not in me but in him who sent me.
verses.kjv: ¶ Jesus cried and said, He that believeth on me, believeth not on me, but on him that sent me.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus cried out, “Whoever believes in Me does not believe in Me alone, but in the One who sent Me.

verses.esv: And whoever sees me sees him who sent me.
verses.kjv: And he that seeth me seeth him that sent me.
verses.bsb: And whoever sees Me sees the One who sent Me.

verses.esv: I have come into the world as light, so that whoever believes in me may not remain in darkness.
verses.kjv: I am come a light into the world, that whosoever believeth on me should not abide in darkness.
verses.bsb: I have come into the world as a light, so that no one who believes in Me should remain in darkness.

verses.esv: If anyone hears my words and does not keep them, I do not judge him; for I did not come to judge the world but to save the world.
verses.kjv: And if any man hear my words, and believe not, I judge him not: for I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.
verses.bsb: As for anyone who hears My words and does not keep them, I do not judge him. For I have not come to judge the world, but to save the world.

verses.esv: The one who rejects me and does not receive my words has a judge; the word that I have spoken will judge him on the last day.
verses.kjv: He that rejecteth me, and receiveth not my words, hath one that judgeth him: the word that I have spoken, the same shall judge him in the last day.
verses.bsb: There is a judge for the one who rejects Me and does not receive My words: The word that I have spoken will judge him on the last day.

verses.esv: For I have not spoken on my own authority, but the Father who sent me has himself given me a commandment—what to say and what to speak.
verses.kjv: For I have not spoken of myself; but the Father which sent me, he gave me a commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak.
verses.bsb: I have not spoken on My own, but the Father who sent Me has commanded Me what to say and how to say it.

verses.esv: And I know that his commandment is eternal life. What I say, therefore, I say as the Father has told me.”
verses.kjv: And I know that his commandment is life everlasting: whatsoever I speak therefore, even as the Father said unto me, so I speak.
verses.bsb: And I know that His command leads to eternal life. So I speak exactly what the Father has told Me to say.”

verses.esv: Now before the Feast of the Passover, when Jesus knew that his hour had come to depart out of this world to the Father, having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them to the end.
verses.kjv: NOW before the feast of the passover, when Jesus knew that his hour was come that he should depart out of this world unto the Father, having loved his own which were in the world, he loved them unto the end.
verses.bsb: It was now just before the Passover Feast, and Jesus knew that His hour had come to leave this world and return to the Father. Having loved His own who were in the world, He loved them to the very end.

verses.esv: During supper, when the devil had already put it into the heart of Judas Iscariot, Simon's son, to betray him,
verses.kjv: And supper being ended, the devil having now put into the heart of Judas Iscariot, Simon’s son, to betray him;
verses.bsb: The evening meal was underway, and the devil had already put into the heart of Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, to betray Jesus.

verses.esv: Jesus, knowing that the Father had given all things into his hands, and that he had come from God and was going back to God,
verses.kjv: Jesus knowing that the Father had given all things into his hands, and that he was come from God, and went to God;
verses.bsb: Jesus knew that the Father had delivered all things into His hands, and that He had come from God and was returning to God.

verses.esv: rose from supper. He laid aside his outer garments, and taking a towel, tied it around his waist.
verses.kjv: He riseth from supper, and laid aside his garments; and took a towel, and girded himself.
verses.bsb: So He got up from the supper, laid aside His outer garments, and wrapped a towel around His waist.

verses.esv: Then he poured water into a basin and began to wash the disciples' feet and to wipe them with the towel that was wrapped around him.
verses.kjv: After that he poureth water into a bason, and began to wash the disciples’ feet, and to wipe them with the towel wherewith he was girded.
verses.bsb: After that, He poured water into a basin and began to wash the disciples’ feet and dry them with the towel that was around Him.

verses.esv: He came to Simon Peter, who said to him, “Lord, do you wash my feet?”
verses.kjv: Then cometh he to Simon Peter: and Peter saith unto him, Lord, dost thou wash my feet?
verses.bsb: He came to Simon Peter, who asked Him, “Lord, are You going to wash my feet?”

verses.esv: Jesus answered him, “What I am doing you do not understand now, but afterward you will understand.”
verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto him, What I do thou knowest not now; but thou shalt know hereafter.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “You do not realize now what I am doing, but later you will understand.”

verses.esv: Peter said to him, “You shall never wash my feet.” Jesus answered him, “If I do not wash you, you have no share with me.”
verses.kjv: Peter saith unto him, Thou shalt never wash my feet. Jesus answered him, If I wash thee not, thou hast no part with me.
verses.bsb: “Never shall You wash my feet!” Peter told Him.  Jesus answered, “Unless I wash you, you have no part with Me.”

verses.esv: Simon Peter said to him, “Lord, not my feet only but also my hands and my head!”
verses.kjv: Simon Peter saith unto him, Lord, not my feet only, but also my hands and my head.
verses.bsb: “Then, Lord,” Simon Peter replied, “not only my feet, but my hands and my head as well!”

verses.esv: Jesus said to him, “The one who has bathed does not need to wash, except for his feet, but is completely clean. And you are clean, but not every one of you.”
verses.kjv: Jesus saith to him, He that is washed needeth not save to wash his feet, but is clean every whit: and ye are clean, but not all.
verses.bsb: Jesus told him, “Whoever has already bathed needs only to wash his feet, and he will be completely clean. And you are clean, though not all of you.”

verses.esv: For he knew who was to betray him; that was why he said, “Not all of you are clean.”
verses.kjv: For he knew who should betray him; therefore said he, Ye are not all clean.
verses.bsb: For He knew who would betray Him. That is why He said, “Not all of you are clean.”

verses.esv: When he had washed their feet and put on his outer garments and resumed his place, he said to them, “Do you understand what I have done to you?
verses.kjv: So after he had washed their feet, and had taken his garments, and was set down again, he said unto them, Know ye what I have done to you?
verses.bsb: When Jesus had washed their feet and put on His outer garments, He reclined with them again and asked, “Do you know what I have done for you?

verses.esv: You call me Teacher and Lord, and you are right, for so I am.
verses.kjv: Ye call me Master and Lord: and ye say well; for so I am.
verses.bsb: You call Me Teacher and Lord, and rightly so, because I am.

verses.esv: If I then, your Lord and Teacher, have washed your feet, you also ought to wash one another's feet.
verses.kjv: If I then, your Lord and Master, have washed your feet; ye also ought to wash one another’s feet.
verses.bsb: So if I, your Lord and Teacher, have washed your feet, you also should wash one another’s feet.

verses.esv: For I have given you an example, that you also should do just as I have done to you.
verses.kjv: For I have given you an example, that ye should do as I have done to you.
verses.bsb: I have set you an example so that you should do as I have done for you.

verses.esv: Truly, truly, I say to you, a servant is not greater than his master, nor is a messenger greater than the one who sent him.
verses.kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord; neither he that is sent greater than he that sent him.
verses.bsb: Truly, truly, I tell you, no servant is greater than his master, nor is a messenger greater than the one who sent him.

verses.esv: If you know these things, blessed are you if you do them.
verses.kjv: If ye know these things, happy are ye if ye do them.
verses.bsb: If you know these things, you will be blessed if you do them.

verses.esv: I am not speaking of all of you; I know whom I have chosen. But the Scripture will be fulfilled, ‘He who ate my bread has lifted his heel against me.’
verses.kjv: ¶ I speak not of you all: I know whom I have chosen: but that the scripture may be fulfilled, He that eateth bread with me hath lifted up his heel against me.
verses.bsb: I am not speaking about all of you; I know whom I have chosen. But this is to fulfill the Scripture: ‘The one who shares My bread has lifted up his heel against Me.’

verses.esv: I am telling you this now, before it takes place, that when it does take place you may believe that I am he.
verses.kjv: Now I tell you before it come, that, when it is come to pass, ye may believe that I am he.
verses.bsb: I am telling you now before it happens, so that when it comes to pass, you will believe that I am He.

verses.esv: Truly, truly, I say to you, whoever receives the one I send receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me.”
verses.kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that receiveth whomsoever I send receiveth me; and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.
verses.bsb: Truly, truly, I tell you, whoever receives the one I send receives Me, and whoever receives Me receives the One who sent Me.”

verses.esv: After saying these things, Jesus was troubled in his spirit, and testified, “Truly, truly, I say to you, one of you will betray me.”
verses.kjv: When Jesus had thus said, he was troubled in spirit, and testified, and said, Verily, verily, I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.
verses.bsb: After Jesus had said this, He became troubled in spirit and testified, “Truly, truly, I tell you, one of you will betray Me.”

verses.esv: The disciples looked at one another, uncertain of whom he spoke.
verses.kjv: Then the disciples looked one on another, doubting of whom he spake.
verses.bsb: The disciples looked at one another, perplexed as to which of them He meant.

verses.esv: One of his disciples, whom Jesus loved, was reclining at table at Jesus' side,
verses.kjv: Now there was leaning on Jesus’ bosom one of his disciples, whom Jesus loved.
verses.bsb: One of His disciples, the one whom Jesus loved, was reclining at His side.

verses.esv: so Simon Peter motioned to him to ask Jesus of whom he was speaking.
verses.kjv: Simon Peter therefore beckoned to him, that he should ask who it should be of whom he spake.
verses.bsb: So Simon Peter motioned to him to ask Jesus which one He was talking about.

verses.esv: So that disciple, leaning back against Jesus, said to him, “Lord, who is it?”
verses.kjv: He then lying on Jesus’ breast saith unto him, Lord, who is it?
verses.bsb: Leaning back against Jesus, he asked, “Lord, who is it?”

verses.esv: Jesus answered, “It is he to whom I will give this morsel of bread when I have dipped it.” So when he had dipped the morsel, he gave it to Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot.
verses.kjv: Jesus answered, He it is, to whom I shall give a sop, when I have dipped it. And when he had dipped the sop, he gave it to Judas Iscariot, the son of Simon.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “It is the one to whom I give this morsel after I have dipped it.” Then He dipped the morsel and gave it to Judas son of Simon Iscariot.

verses.esv: Then after he had taken the morsel, Satan entered into him. Jesus said to him, “What you are going to do, do quickly.”
verses.kjv: And after the sop Satan entered into him. Then said Jesus unto him, That thou doest, do quickly.
verses.bsb: And when Judas had taken the morsel, Satan entered into him.  Then Jesus said to Judas, “What you are about to do, do quickly.”

verses.esv: Now no one at the table knew why he said this to him.
verses.kjv: Now no man at the table knew for what intent he spake this unto him.
verses.bsb: But no one at the table knew why Jesus had said this to him.

verses.esv: Some thought that, because Judas had the moneybag, Jesus was telling him, “Buy what we need for the feast,” or that he should give something to the poor.
verses.kjv: For some of them thought, because Judas had the bag, that Jesus had said unto him, Buy those things that we have need of against the feast; or, that he should give something to the poor.
verses.bsb: Since Judas kept the money bag, some thought that Jesus was telling him to buy what was needed for the feast, or to give something to the poor.

verses.esv: So, after receiving the morsel of bread, he immediately went out. And it was night.
verses.kjv: He then having received the sop went immediately out: and it was night.
verses.bsb: As soon as he had received the morsel, Judas went out into the night.

verses.esv: When he had gone out, Jesus said, “Now is the Son of Man glorified, and God is glorified in him.
verses.kjv: ¶ Therefore, when he was gone out, Jesus said, Now is the Son of man glorified, and God is glorified in him.
verses.bsb: When Judas had gone out, Jesus said, “Now the Son of Man is glorified, and God is glorified in Him.

verses.esv: If God is glorified in him, God will also glorify him in himself, and glorify him at once.
verses.kjv: If God be glorified in him, God shall also glorify him in himself, and shall straightway glorify him.
verses.bsb: If God is glorified in Him, God will also glorify the Son in Himself—and will glorify Him at once.

verses.esv: Little children, yet a little while I am with you. You will seek me, and just as I said to the Jews, so now I also say to you, ‘Where I am going you cannot come.’
verses.kjv: Little children, yet a little while I am with you. Ye shall seek me: and as I said unto the Jews, Whither I go, ye cannot come; so now I say to you.
verses.bsb: Little children, I am with you only a little while longer. You will look for Me, and as I said to the Jews, so now I say to you: ‘Where I am going, you cannot come.’

verses.esv: A new commandment I give to you, that you love one another: just as I have loved you, you also are to love one another.
verses.kjv: A new commandment I give unto you, That ye love one another; as I have loved you, that ye also love one another.
verses.bsb: A new commandment I give you: Love one another. As I have loved you, so you also must love one another.

verses.esv: By this all people will know that you are my disciples, if you have love for one another.”
verses.kjv: By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples, if ye have love one to another.
verses.bsb: By this everyone will know that you are My disciples, if you love one another.”

verses.esv: Simon Peter said to him, “Lord, where are you going?” Jesus answered him, “Where I am going you cannot follow me now, but you will follow afterward.”
verses.kjv: ¶ Simon Peter said unto him, Lord, whither goest thou? Jesus answered him, Whither I go, thou canst not follow me now; but thou shalt follow me afterwards.
verses.bsb: “Lord, where are You going?” Simon Peter asked.  Jesus answered, “Where I am going, you cannot follow Me now, but you will follow later.”

verses.esv: Peter said to him, “Lord, why can I not follow you now? I will lay down my life for you.”
verses.kjv: Peter said unto him, Lord, why cannot I follow thee now? I will lay down my life for thy sake.
verses.bsb: “Lord,” said Peter, “why can’t I follow You now? I will lay down my life for You.”

verses.esv: Jesus answered, “Will you lay down your life for me? Truly, truly, I say to you, the rooster will not crow till you have denied me three times.
verses.kjv: Jesus answered him, Wilt thou lay down thy life for my sake? Verily, verily, I say unto thee, The cock shall not crow, till thou hast denied me thrice.
verses.bsb: “Will you lay down your life for Me?” Jesus replied. “Truly, truly, I tell you, before the rooster crows, you will deny Me three times.

verses.esv: “Let not your hearts be troubled. Believe in God; believe also in me.
verses.kjv: LET not your heart be troubled: ye believe in God, believe also in me.
verses.bsb: “Do not let your hearts be troubled. You believe in God; believe in Me as well.

verses.esv: In my Father's house are many rooms. If it were not so, would I have told you that I go to prepare a place for you?
verses.kjv: In my Father’s house are many mansions: if it were not so, I would have told you. I go to prepare a place for you.
verses.bsb: In My Father’s house are many rooms. If it were not so, would I have told you that I am going there to prepare a place for you?

verses.esv: And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again and will take you to myself, that where I am you may be also.
verses.kjv: And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and receive you unto myself; that where I am, there ye may be also.
verses.bsb: And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come back and welcome you into My presence, so that you also may be where I am.

verses.esv: And you know the way to where I am going.”
verses.kjv: And whither I go ye know, and the way ye know.
verses.bsb: You know the way to the place where I am going.”

verses.esv: Thomas said to him, “Lord, we do not know where you are going. How can we know the way?”
verses.kjv: Thomas saith unto him, Lord, we know not whither thou goest; and how can we know the way?
verses.bsb: “Lord,” said Thomas, “we do not know where You are going, so how can we know the way?”

verses.esv: Jesus said to him, “I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through Me.

verses.esv: If you had known me, you would have known my Father also. From now on you do know him and have seen him.”
verses.kjv: If ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also: and from henceforth ye know him, and have seen him.
verses.bsb: If you had known Me, you would know My Father as well. From now on you do know Him and have seen Him.”

verses.esv: Philip said to him, “Lord, show us the Father, and it is enough for us.”
verses.kjv: Philip saith unto him, Lord, shew us the Father, and it sufficeth us.
verses.bsb: Philip said to Him, “Lord, show us the Father, and that will be enough for us.”

verses.esv: Jesus said to him, “Have I been with you so long, and you still do not know me, Philip? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, ‘Show us the Father’?
verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto him, Have I been so long time with you, and yet hast thou not known me, Philip? he that hath seen me hath seen the Father; and how sayest thou then, Shew us the Father?
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Philip, I have been with you all this time, and still you do not know Me? Anyone who has seen Me has seen the Father. How can you say, ‘Show us the Father’?

verses.esv: Do you not believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words that I say to you I do not speak on my own authority, but the Father who dwells in me does his works.
verses.kjv: Believest thou not that I am in the Father, and the Father in me? the words that I speak unto you I speak not of myself: but the Father that dwelleth in me, he doeth the works.
verses.bsb: Do you not believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in Me? The words I say to you, I do not speak on My own. Instead, it is the Father dwelling in Me, performing His works.

verses.esv: Believe me that I am in the Father and the Father is in me, or else believe on account of the works themselves.
verses.kjv: Believe me that I am in the Father, and the Father in me: or else believe me for the very works’ sake.
verses.bsb: Believe Me that I am in the Father and the Father is in Me—or at least believe on account of the works themselves.

verses.esv: “Truly, truly, I say to you, whoever believes in me will also do the works that I do; and greater works than these will he do, because I am going to the Father.
verses.kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth on me, the works that I do shall he do also; and greater works than these shall he do; because I go unto my Father.
verses.bsb: Truly, truly, I tell you, whoever believes in Me will also do the works that I am doing. He will do even greater things than these, because I am going to the Father.

verses.esv: Whatever you ask in my name, this I will do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
verses.kjv: And whatsoever ye shall ask in my name, that will I do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
verses.bsb: And I will do whatever you ask in My name, so that the Father may be glorified in the Son.

verses.esv: If you ask me anything in my name, I will do it.
verses.kjv: If ye shall ask any thing in my name, I will do it.
verses.bsb: If you ask Me for anything in My name, I will do it.

verses.esv: “If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
verses.kjv: ¶ If ye love me, keep my commandments.
verses.bsb: If you love Me, you will keep My commandments.

verses.esv: And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Helper, to be with you forever,
verses.kjv: And I will pray the Father, and he shall give you another Comforter, that he may abide with you for ever;
verses.bsb: And I will ask the Father, and He will give you another Advocate to be with you forever—

verses.esv: even the Spirit of truth, whom the world cannot receive, because it neither sees him nor knows him. You know him, for he dwells with you and will be in you.
verses.kjv: Even the Spirit of truth; whom the world cannot receive, because it seeth him not, neither knoweth him: but ye know him; for he dwelleth with you, and shall be in you.
verses.bsb: the Spirit of truth. The world cannot receive Him, because it neither sees Him nor knows Him. But you do know Him, for He abides with you and will be in you.

verses.esv: “I will not leave you as orphans; I will come to you.
verses.kjv: I will not leave you comfortless: I will come to you.
verses.bsb: I will not leave you as orphans; I will come to you.

verses.esv: Yet a little while and the world will see me no more, but you will see me. Because I live, you also will live.
verses.kjv: Yet a little while, and the world seeth me no more; but ye see me: because I live, ye shall live also.
verses.bsb: In a little while the world will see Me no more, but you will see Me. Because I live, you also will live.

verses.esv: In that day you will know that I am in my Father, and you in me, and I in you.
verses.kjv: At that day ye shall know that I am in my Father, and ye in me, and I in you.
verses.bsb: On that day you will know that I am in My Father, and you are in Me, and I am in you.

verses.esv: Whoever has my commandments and keeps them, he it is who loves me. And he who loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love him and manifest myself to him.”
verses.kjv: He that hath my commandments, and keepeth them, he it is that loveth me: and he that loveth me shall be loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will manifest myself to him.
verses.bsb: Whoever has My commandments and keeps them is the one who loves Me. The one who loves Me will be loved by My Father, and I will love him and reveal Myself to him.”

verses.esv: Judas (not Iscariot) said to him, “Lord, how is it that you will manifest yourself to us, and not to the world?”
verses.kjv: Judas saith unto him, not Iscariot, Lord, how is it that thou wilt manifest thyself unto us, and not unto the world?
verses.bsb: Judas (not Iscariot) asked Him, “Lord, why are You going to reveal Yourself to us and not to the world?”

verses.esv: Jesus answered him, “If anyone loves me, he will keep my word, and my Father will love him, and we will come to him and make our home with him.
verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto him, If a man love me, he will keep my words: and my Father will love him, and we will come unto him, and make our abode with him.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “If anyone loves Me, he will keep My word. My Father will love him, and We will come to him and make Our home with him.

verses.esv: Whoever does not love me does not keep my words. And the word that you hear is not mine but the Father's who sent me.
verses.kjv: He that loveth me not keepeth not my sayings: and the word which ye hear is not mine, but the Father’s which sent me.
verses.bsb: Whoever does not love Me does not keep My words. The word that you hear is not My own, but it is from the Father who sent Me.

verses.esv: “These things I have spoken to you while I am still with you.
verses.kjv: These things have I spoken unto you, being yet present with you.
verses.bsb: All this I have spoken to you while I am still with you.

verses.esv: But the Helper, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, he will teach you all things and bring to your remembrance all that I have said to you.
verses.kjv: But the Comforter, which is the Holy Ghost, whom the Father will send in my name, he shall teach you all things, and bring all things to your remembrance, whatsoever I have said unto you.
verses.bsb: But the Advocate, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in My name, will teach you all things and will remind you of everything I have told you.

verses.esv: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. Not as the world gives do I give to you. Let not your hearts be troubled, neither let them be afraid.
verses.kjv: Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.
verses.bsb: Peace I leave with you; My peace I give to you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled; do not be afraid.

verses.esv: You heard me say to you, ‘I am going away, and I will come to you.’ If you loved me, you would have rejoiced, because I am going to the Father, for the Father is greater than I.
verses.kjv: Ye have heard how I said unto you, I go away, and come again unto you. If ye loved me, ye would rejoice, because I said, I go unto the Father: for my Father is greater than I.
verses.bsb: You heard Me say, ‘I am going away, and I am coming back to you.’ If you loved Me, you would rejoice that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than I.

verses.esv: And now I have told you before it takes place, so that when it does take place you may believe.
verses.kjv: And now I have told you before it come to pass, that, when it is come to pass, ye might believe.
verses.bsb: And now I have told you before it happens, so that when it does happen, you will believe.

verses.esv: I will no longer talk much with you, for the ruler of this world is coming. He has no claim on me,
verses.kjv: Hereafter I will not talk much with you: for the prince of this world cometh, and hath nothing in me.
verses.bsb: I will not speak with you much longer, for the prince of this world is coming, and he has no claim on Me.

verses.esv: but I do as the Father has commanded me, so that the world may know that I love the Father. Rise, let us go from here.
verses.kjv: But that the world may know that I love the Father; and as the Father gave me commandment, even so I do. Arise, let us go hence.
verses.bsb: But I do exactly what the Father has commanded Me, so that the world may know that I love the Father.  Get up! Let us go on from here.

verses.esv: “I am the true vine, and my Father is the vinedresser.
verses.kjv: I AM the true vine, and my Father is the husbandman.
verses.bsb: “I am the true vine, and My Father is the keeper of the vineyard.

verses.esv: Every branch in me that does not bear fruit he takes away, and every branch that does bear fruit he prunes, that it may bear more fruit.
verses.kjv: Every branch in me that beareth not fruit he taketh away: and every branch that beareth fruit, he purgeth it, that it may bring forth more fruit.
verses.bsb: He cuts off every branch in Me that bears no fruit, and every branch that does bear fruit, He prunes to make it even more fruitful.

verses.esv: Already you are clean because of the word that I have spoken to you.
verses.kjv: Now ye are clean through the word which I have spoken unto you.
verses.bsb: You are already clean because of the word I have spoken to you.

verses.esv: Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit by itself, unless it abides in the vine, neither can you, unless you abide in me.
verses.kjv: Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; no more can ye, except ye abide in me.
verses.bsb: Remain in Me, and I will remain in you. Just as no branch can bear fruit by itself unless it remains in the vine, neither can you bear fruit unless you remain in Me.

verses.esv: I am the vine; you are the branches. Whoever abides in me and I in him, he it is that bears much fruit, for apart from me you can do nothing.
verses.kjv: I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do nothing.
verses.bsb: I am the vine and you are the branches. The one who remains in Me, and I in him, will bear much fruit. For apart from Me you can do nothing.

verses.esv: If anyone does not abide in me he is thrown away like a branch and withers; and the branches are gathered, thrown into the fire, and burned.
verses.kjv: If a man abide not in me, he is cast forth as a branch, and is withered; and men gather them, and cast them into the fire, and they are burned.
verses.bsb: If anyone does not remain in Me, he is like a branch that is thrown away and withers. Such branches are gathered up, thrown into the fire, and burned.

verses.esv: If you abide in me, and my words abide in you, ask whatever you wish, and it will be done for you.
verses.kjv: If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you.
verses.bsb: If you remain in Me and My words remain in you, ask whatever you wish, and it will be done for you.

verses.esv: By this my Father is glorified, that you bear much fruit and so prove to be my disciples.
verses.kjv: Herein is my Father glorified, that ye bear much fruit; so shall ye be my disciples.
verses.bsb: This is to My Father’s glory, that you bear much fruit, proving yourselves to be My disciples.

verses.esv: As the Father has loved me, so have I loved you. Abide in my love.
verses.kjv: As the Father hath loved me, so have I loved you: continue ye in my love.
verses.bsb: As the Father has loved Me, so have I loved you. Remain in My love.

verses.esv: If you keep my commandments, you will abide in my love, just as I have kept my Father's commandments and abide in his love.
verses.kjv: If ye keep my commandments, ye shall abide in my love; even as I have kept my Father’s commandments, and abide in his love.
verses.bsb: If you keep My commandments, you will remain in My love, just as I have kept My Father’s commandments and remain in His love.

verses.esv: These things I have spoken to you, that my joy may be in you, and that your joy may be full.
verses.kjv: These things have I spoken unto you, that my joy might remain in you, and that your joy might be full.
verses.bsb: I have told you these things so that My joy may be in you and your joy may be complete.

verses.esv: “This is my commandment, that you love one another as I have loved you.
verses.kjv: This is my commandment, That ye love one another, as I have loved you.
verses.bsb: This is My commandment, that you love one another as I have loved you.

verses.esv: Greater love has no one than this, that someone lay down his life for his friends.
verses.kjv: Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends.
verses.bsb: Greater love has no one than this, that he lay down his life for his friends.

verses.esv: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
verses.kjv: Ye are my friends, if ye do whatsoever I command you.
verses.bsb: You are My friends if you do what I command you.

verses.esv: No longer do I call you servants, for the servant does not know what his master is doing; but I have called you friends, for all that I have heard from my Father I have made known to you.
verses.kjv: Henceforth I call you not servants; for the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth: but I have called you friends; for all things that I have heard of my Father I have made known unto you.
verses.bsb: No longer do I call you servants, for a servant does not understand what his master is doing. But I have called you friends, because everything I have learned from My Father I have made known to you.

verses.esv: You did not choose me, but I chose you and appointed you that you should go and bear fruit and that your fruit should abide, so that whatever you ask the Father in my name, he may give it to you.
verses.kjv: Ye have not chosen me, but I have chosen you, and ordained you, that ye should go and bring forth fruit, and that your fruit should remain: that whatsoever ye shall ask of the Father in my name, he may give it you.
verses.bsb: You did not choose Me, but I chose you. And I appointed you to go and bear fruit—fruit that will remain—so that whatever you ask the Father in My name, He will give you.

verses.esv: These things I command you, so that you will love one another.
verses.kjv: These things I command you, that ye love one another.
verses.bsb: This is My command to you: Love one another.

verses.esv: “If the world hates you, know that it has hated me before it hated you.
verses.kjv: If the world hate you, ye know that it hated me before it hated you.
verses.bsb: If the world hates you, understand that it hated Me first.

verses.esv: If you were of the world, the world would love you as its own; but because you are not of the world, but I chose you out of the world, therefore the world hates you.
verses.kjv: If ye were of the world, the world would love his own: but because ye are not of the world, but I have chosen you out of the world, therefore the world hateth you.
verses.bsb: If you were of the world, it would love you as its own. Instead, the world hates you, because you are not of the world, but I have chosen you out of the world.

verses.esv: Remember the word that I said to you: ‘A servant is not greater than his master.’ If they persecuted me, they will also persecute you. If they kept my word, they will also keep yours.
verses.kjv: Remember the word that I said unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord. If they have persecuted me, they will also persecute you; if they have kept my saying, they will keep yours also.
verses.bsb: Remember the word that I spoke to you: ‘No servant is greater than his master.’ If they persecuted Me, they will persecute you as well; if they kept My word, they will keep yours as well.

verses.esv: But all these things they will do to you on account of my name, because they do not know him who sent me.
verses.kjv: But all these things will they do unto you for my name’s sake, because they know not him that sent me.
verses.bsb: But they will treat you like this because of My name, since they do not know the One who sent Me.

verses.esv: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not have been guilty of sin, but now they have no excuse for their sin.
verses.kjv: If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had sin: but now they have no cloke for their sin.
verses.bsb: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. Now, however, they have no excuse for their sin.

verses.esv: Whoever hates me hates my Father also.
verses.kjv: He that hateth me hateth my Father also.
verses.bsb: Whoever hates Me hates My Father as well.

verses.esv: If I had not done among them the works that no one else did, they would not be guilty of sin, but now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
verses.kjv: If I had not done among them the works which none other man did, they had not had sin: but now have they both seen and hated both me and my Father.
verses.bsb: If I had not done among them the works that no one else did, they would not be guilty of sin; but now they have seen and hated both Me and My Father.

verses.esv: But the word that is written in their Law must be fulfilled: ‘They hated me without a cause.’
verses.kjv: But this cometh to pass, that the word might be fulfilled that is written in their law, They hated me without a cause.
verses.bsb: But this is to fulfill what is written in their Law: ‘They hated Me without reason.’

verses.esv: “But when the Helper comes, whom I will send to you from the Father, the Spirit of truth, who proceeds from the Father, he will bear witness about me.
verses.kjv: But when the Comforter is come, whom I will send unto you from the Father, even the Spirit of truth, which proceedeth from the Father, he shall testify of me:
verses.bsb: When the Advocate comes, whom I will send to you from the Father—the Spirit of truth who proceeds from the Father—He will testify about Me.

verses.esv: And you also will bear witness, because you have been with me from the beginning.
verses.kjv: And ye also shall bear witness, because ye have been with me from the beginning.
verses.bsb: And you also must testify, because you have been with Me from the beginning.

verses.esv: “I have said all these things to you to keep you from falling away.
verses.kjv: THESE things have I spoken unto you, that ye should not be offended.
verses.bsb: “I have told you these things so that you will not fall away.

verses.esv: They will put you out of the synagogues. Indeed, the hour is coming when whoever kills you will think he is offering service to God.
verses.kjv: They shall put you out of the synagogues: yea, the time cometh, that whosoever killeth you will think that he doeth God service.
verses.bsb: They will put you out of the synagogues. In fact, a time is coming when anyone who kills you will think he is offering a service to God.

verses.esv: And they will do these things because they have not known the Father, nor me.
verses.kjv: And these things will they do unto you, because they have not known the Father, nor me.
verses.bsb: They will do these things because they have not known the Father or Me.

verses.esv: But I have said these things to you, that when their hour comes you may remember that I told them to you. “I did not say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
verses.kjv: But these things have I told you, that when the time shall come, ye may remember that I told you of them. And these things I said not unto you at the beginning, because I was with you.
