verses.bsb: But I have told you these things so that when their hour comes, you will remember that I told you about them. I did not tell you these things from the beginning, because I was with you.

verses.esv: But now I am going to him who sent me, and none of you asks me, ‘Where are you going?’
verses.kjv: But now I go my way to him that sent me; and none of you asketh me, Whither goest thou?
verses.bsb: Now, however, I am going to Him who sent Me; yet none of you asks Me, ‘Where are You going?’

verses.esv: But because I have said these things to you, sorrow has filled your heart.
verses.kjv: But because I have said these things unto you, sorrow hath filled your heart.
verses.bsb: Instead, your hearts are filled with sorrow because I have told you these things.

verses.esv: Nevertheless, I tell you the truth: it is to your advantage that I go away, for if I do not go away, the Helper will not come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
verses.kjv: Nevertheless I tell you the truth; It is expedient for you that I go away: for if I go not away, the Comforter will not come unto you; but if I depart, I will send him unto you.
verses.bsb: But I tell you the truth, it is for your benefit that I am going away. Unless I go away, the Advocate will not come to you; but if I go, I will send Him to you.

verses.esv: And when he comes, he will convict the world concerning sin and righteousness and judgment:
verses.kjv: And when he is come, he will reprove the world of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment:
verses.bsb: And when He comes, He will convict the world in regard to sin and righteousness and judgment:

verses.esv: concerning sin, because they do not believe in me;
verses.kjv: Of sin, because they believe not on me;
verses.bsb: in regard to sin, because they do not believe in Me;

verses.esv: concerning righteousness, because I go to the Father, and you will see me no longer;
verses.kjv: Of righteousness, because I go to my Father, and ye see me no more;
verses.bsb: in regard to righteousness, because I am going to the Father and you will no longer see Me;

verses.esv: concerning judgment, because the ruler of this world is judged.
verses.kjv: Of judgment, because the prince of this world is judged.
verses.bsb: and in regard to judgment, because the prince of this world has been condemned.

verses.esv: “I still have many things to say to you, but you cannot bear them now.
verses.kjv: I have yet many things to say unto you, but ye cannot bear them now.
verses.bsb: I still have much to tell you, but you cannot yet bear to hear it.

verses.esv: When the Spirit of truth comes, he will guide you into all the truth, for he will not speak on his own authority, but whatever he hears he will speak, and he will declare to you the things that are to come.
verses.kjv: Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you into all truth: for he shall not speak of himself; but whatsoever he shall hear, that shall he speak: and he will shew you things to come.
verses.bsb: However, when the Spirit of truth comes, He will guide you into all truth. For He will not speak on His own, but He will speak what He hears, and He will declare to you what is to come.

verses.esv: He will glorify me, for he will take what is mine and declare it to you.
verses.kjv: He shall glorify me: for he shall receive of mine, and shall shew it unto you.
verses.bsb: He will glorify Me by taking from what is Mine and disclosing it to you.

verses.esv: All that the Father has is mine; therefore I said that he will take what is mine and declare it to you.
verses.kjv: All things that the Father hath are mine: therefore said I, that he shall take of mine, and shall shew it unto you.
verses.bsb: Everything that belongs to the Father is Mine. That is why I said that the Spirit will take from what is Mine and disclose it to you.

verses.esv: “A little while, and you will see me no longer; and again a little while, and you will see me.”
verses.kjv: A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me, because I go to the Father.
verses.bsb: In a little while you will see Me no more, and then after a little while you will see Me.”

verses.esv: So some of his disciples said to one another, “What is this that he says to us, ‘A little while, and you will not see me, and again a little while, and you will see me’; and, ‘because I am going to the Father’?”
verses.kjv: Then said some of his disciples among themselves, What is this that he saith unto us, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me: and, Because I go to the Father?
verses.bsb: Then some of His disciples asked one another, “Why is He telling us, ‘In a little while you will not see Me, and then after a little while you will see Me’ and ‘Because I am going to the Father’?”

verses.esv: So they were saying, “What does he mean by ‘a little while’? We do not know what he is talking about.”
verses.kjv: They said therefore, What is this that he saith, A little while? we cannot tell what he saith.
verses.bsb: They kept asking, “Why is He saying, ‘a little while’? We do not understand what He is saying.”

verses.esv: Jesus knew that they wanted to ask him, so he said to them, “Is this what you are asking yourselves, what I meant by saying, ‘A little while and you will not see me, and again a little while and you will see me’?
verses.kjv: Now Jesus knew that they were desirous to ask him, and said unto them, Do ye inquire among yourselves of that I said, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me?
verses.bsb: Aware that they wanted to question Him, Jesus said to them, “Are you asking one another why I said, ‘In a little while you will not see Me, and then after a little while you will see Me’?

verses.esv: Truly, truly, I say to you, you will weep and lament, but the world will rejoice. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn into joy.
verses.kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto you, That ye shall weep and lament, but the world shall rejoice: and ye shall be sorrowful, but your sorrow shall be turned into joy.
verses.bsb: Truly, truly, I tell you, you will weep and wail while the world rejoices. You will grieve, but your grief will turn to joy.

verses.esv: When a woman is giving birth, she has sorrow because her hour has come, but when she has delivered the baby, she no longer remembers the anguish, for joy that a human being has been born into the world.
verses.kjv: A woman when she is in travail hath sorrow, because her hour is come: but as soon as she is delivered of the child, she remembereth no more the anguish, for joy that a man is born into the world.
verses.bsb: A woman has pain in childbirth because her time has come; but when she brings forth her child, she forgets her anguish because of her joy that a child has been born into the world.

verses.esv: So also you have sorrow now, but I will see you again, and your hearts will rejoice, and no one will take your joy from you.
verses.kjv: And ye now therefore have sorrow: but I will see you again, and your heart shall rejoice, and your joy no man taketh from you.
verses.bsb: So also you have sorrow now, but I will see you again and your hearts will rejoice, and no one will take away your joy.

verses.esv: In that day you will ask nothing of me. Truly, truly, I say to you, whatever you ask of the Father in my name, he will give it to you.
verses.kjv: And in that day ye shall ask me nothing. Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whatsoever ye shall ask the Father in my name, he will give it you.
verses.bsb: In that day you will no longer ask Me anything. Truly, truly, I tell you, whatever you ask the Father in My name, He will give you.

verses.esv: Until now you have asked nothing in my name. Ask, and you will receive, that your joy may be full.
verses.kjv: Hitherto have ye asked nothing in my name: ask, and ye shall receive, that your joy may be full.
verses.bsb: Until now you have not asked for anything in My name. Ask and you will receive, so that your joy may be complete.

verses.esv: “I have said these things to you in figures of speech. The hour is coming when I will no longer speak to you in figures of speech but will tell you plainly about the Father.
verses.kjv: These things have I spoken unto you in proverbs: but the time cometh, when I shall no more speak unto you in proverbs, but I shall shew you plainly of the Father.
verses.bsb: I have spoken these things to you in figures of speech. An hour is coming when I will no longer speak to you this way, but will tell you plainly about the Father.

verses.esv: In that day you will ask in my name, and I do not say to you that I will ask the Father on your behalf;
verses.kjv: At that day ye shall ask in my name: and I say not unto you, that I will pray the Father for you:
verses.bsb: In that day you will ask in My name. I am not saying that I will ask the Father on your behalf.

verses.esv: for the Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and have believed that I came from God.
verses.kjv: For the Father himself loveth you, because ye have loved me, and have believed that I came out from God.
verses.bsb: For the Father Himself loves you, because you have loved Me and have believed that I came from God.

verses.esv: I came from the Father and have come into the world, and now I am leaving the world and going to the Father.”
verses.kjv: I came forth from the Father, and am come into the world: again, I leave the world, and go to the Father.
verses.bsb: I came from the Father and entered the world. In turn, I will leave the world and go to the Father.”

verses.esv: His disciples said, “Ah, now you are speaking plainly and not using figurative speech!
verses.kjv: His disciples said unto him, Lo, now speakest thou plainly, and speakest no proverb.
verses.bsb: His disciples said, “See, now You are speaking plainly and without figures of speech.

verses.esv: Now we know that you know all things and do not need anyone to question you; this is why we believe that you came from God.”
verses.kjv: Now are we sure that thou knowest all things, and needest not that any man should ask thee: by this we believe that thou camest forth from God.
verses.bsb: Now we understand that You know all things and that You have no need for anyone to question You. Because of this, we believe that You came from God.”

verses.esv: Jesus answered them, “Do you now believe?
verses.kjv: Jesus answered them, Do ye now believe?
verses.bsb: “Do you finally believe?” Jesus replied.

verses.esv: Behold, the hour is coming, indeed it has come, when you will be scattered, each to his own home, and will leave me alone. Yet I am not alone, for the Father is with me.
verses.kjv: Behold, the hour cometh, yea, is now come, that ye shall be scattered, every man to his own, and shall leave me alone: and yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.
verses.bsb: “Look, an hour is coming and has already come when you will be scattered, each to his own home, and you will leave Me all alone. Yet I am not alone, because the Father is with Me.

verses.esv: I have said these things to you, that in me you may have peace. In the world you will have tribulation. But take heart; I have overcome the world.”
verses.kjv: These things I have spoken unto you, that in me ye might have peace. In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.
verses.bsb: I have told you these things so that in Me you may have peace. In the world you will have tribulation. But take courage; I have overcome the world!”

verses.esv: When Jesus had spoken these words, he lifted up his eyes to heaven, and said, “Father, the hour has come; glorify your Son that the Son may glorify you,
verses.kjv: THESE words spake Jesus, and lifted up his eyes to heaven, and said, Father, the hour is come; glorify thy Son, that thy Son also may glorify thee:
verses.bsb: When Jesus had spoken these things, He lifted up His eyes to heaven and said, “Father, the hour has come. Glorify Your Son, that Your Son may glorify You.

verses.esv: since you have given him authority over all flesh, to give eternal life to all whom you have given him.
verses.kjv: As thou hast given him power over all flesh, that he should give eternal life to as many as thou hast given him.
verses.bsb: For You granted Him authority over all people, so that He may give eternal life to all those You have given Him.

verses.esv: And this is eternal life, that they know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you have sent.
verses.kjv: And this is life eternal, that they might know thee the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom thou hast sent.
verses.bsb: Now this is eternal life, that they may know You, the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom You have sent.

verses.esv: I glorified you on earth, having accomplished the work that you gave me to do.
verses.kjv: I have glorified thee on the earth: I have finished the work which thou gavest me to do.
verses.bsb: I have glorified You on earth by accomplishing the work You gave Me to do.

verses.esv: And now, Father, glorify me in your own presence with the glory that I had with you before the world existed.
verses.kjv: And now, O Father, glorify thou me with thine own self with the glory which I had with thee before the world was.
verses.bsb: And now, Father, glorify Me in Your presence with the glory I had with You before the world existed.

verses.esv: “I have manifested your name to the people whom you gave me out of the world. Yours they were, and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
verses.kjv: I have manifested thy name unto the men which thou gavest me out of the world: thine they were, and thou gavest them me; and they have kept thy word.
verses.bsb: I have revealed Your name to those You have given Me out of the world. They were Yours; You gave them to Me, and they have kept Your word.

verses.esv: Now they know that everything that you have given me is from you.
verses.kjv: Now they have known that all things whatsoever thou hast given me are of thee.
verses.bsb: Now they know that everything You have given Me comes from You.

verses.esv: For I have given them the words that you gave me, and they have received them and have come to know in truth that I came from you; and they have believed that you sent me.
verses.kjv: For I have given unto them the words which thou gavest me; and they have received them, and have known surely that I came out from thee, and they have believed that thou didst send me.
verses.bsb: For I have given them the words You gave Me, and they have received them. They knew with certainty that I came from You, and they believed that You sent Me.

verses.esv: I am praying for them. I am not praying for the world but for those whom you have given me, for they are yours.
verses.kjv: I pray for them: I pray not for the world, but for them which thou hast given me; for they are thine.
verses.bsb: I ask on their behalf. I do not ask on behalf of the world, but on behalf of those You have given Me; for they are Yours.

verses.esv: All mine are yours, and yours are mine, and I am glorified in them.
verses.kjv: And all mine are thine, and thine are mine; and I am glorified in them.
verses.bsb: All I have is Yours, and all You have is Mine; and in them I have been glorified.

verses.esv: And I am no longer in the world, but they are in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, keep them in your name, which you have given me, that they may be one, even as we are one.
verses.kjv: And now I am no more in the world, but these are in the world, and I come to thee. Holy Father, keep through thine own name those whom thou hast given me, that they may be one, as we are.
verses.bsb: I will no longer be in the world, but they are in the world, and I am coming to You.  Holy Father, protect them by Your name, the name You gave Me, so that they may be one as We are one.

verses.esv: While I was with them, I kept them in your name, which you have given me. I have guarded them, and not one of them has been lost except the son of destruction, that the Scripture might be fulfilled.
verses.kjv: While I was with them in the world, I kept them in thy name: those that thou gavest me I have kept, and none of them is lost, but the son of perdition; that the scripture might be fulfilled.
verses.bsb: While I was with them, I protected and preserved them by Your name, the name You gave Me. Not one of them has been lost, except the son of destruction, so that the Scripture would be fulfilled.

verses.esv: But now I am coming to you, and these things I speak in the world, that they may have my joy fulfilled in themselves.
verses.kjv: And now come I to thee; and these things I speak in the world, that they might have my joy fulfilled in themselves.
verses.bsb: But now I am coming to You; and I am saying these things while I am in the world, so that they may have My joy fulfilled within them.

verses.esv: I have given them your word, and the world has hated them because they are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.
verses.kjv: I have given them thy word; and the world hath hated them, because they are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.
verses.bsb: I have given them Your word and the world has hated them; for they are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.

verses.esv: I do not ask that you take them out of the world, but that you keep them from the evil one.
verses.kjv: I pray not that thou shouldest take them out of the world, but that thou shouldest keep them from the evil.
verses.bsb: I am not asking that You take them out of the world, but that You keep them from the evil one.

verses.esv: They are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.
verses.kjv: They are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.
verses.bsb: They are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.

verses.esv: Sanctify them in the truth; your word is truth.
verses.kjv: Sanctify them through thy truth: thy word is truth.
verses.bsb: Sanctify them by the truth; Your word is truth.

verses.esv: As you sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
verses.kjv: As thou hast sent me into the world, even so have I also sent them into the world.
verses.bsb: As You sent Me into the world, I have also sent them into the world.

verses.esv: And for their sake I consecrate myself, that they also may be sanctified in truth.
verses.kjv: And for their sakes I sanctify myself, that they also might be sanctified through the truth.
verses.bsb: For them I sanctify Myself, so that they too may be sanctified by the truth.

verses.esv: “I do not ask for these only, but also for those who will believe in me through their word,
verses.kjv: Neither pray I for these alone, but for them also which shall believe on me through their word;
verses.bsb: I am not asking on behalf of them alone, but also on behalf of those who will believe in Me through their message,

verses.esv: that they may all be one, just as you, Father, are in me, and I in you, that they also may be in us, so that the world may believe that you have sent me.
verses.kjv: That they all may be one; as thou, Father, art in me, and I in thee, that they also may be one in us: that the world may believe that thou hast sent me.
verses.bsb: that all of them may be one, as You, Father, are in Me, and I am in You. May they also be in Us, so that the world may believe that You sent Me.

verses.esv: The glory that you have given me I have given to them, that they may be one even as we are one,
verses.kjv: And the glory which thou gavest me I have given them; that they may be one, even as we are one:
verses.bsb: I have given them the glory You gave Me, so that they may be one as We are one—

verses.esv: I in them and you in me, that they may become perfectly one, so that the world may know that you sent me and loved them even as you loved me.
verses.kjv: I in them, and thou in me, that they may be made perfect in one; and that the world may know that thou hast sent me, and hast loved them, as thou hast loved me.
verses.bsb: I in them and You in Me—that they may be perfectly united, so that the world may know that You sent Me and have loved them just as You have loved Me.

verses.esv: Father, I desire that they also, whom you have given me, may be with me where I am, to see my glory that you have given me because you loved me before the foundation of the world.
verses.kjv: Father, I will that they also, whom thou hast given me, be with me where I am; that they may behold my glory, which thou hast given me: for thou lovedst me before the foundation of the world.
verses.bsb: Father, I want those You have given Me to be with Me where I am, that they may see the glory You gave Me because You loved Me before the foundation of the world.

verses.esv: O righteous Father, even though the world does not know you, I know you, and these know that you have sent me.
verses.kjv: O righteous Father, the world hath not known thee: but I have known thee, and these have known that thou hast sent me.
verses.bsb: Righteous Father, although the world has not known You, I know You, and they know that You sent Me.

verses.esv: I made known to them your name, and I will continue to make it known, that the love with which you have loved me may be in them, and I in them.”
verses.kjv: And I have declared unto them thy name, and will declare it: that the love wherewith thou hast loved me may be in them, and I in them.
verses.bsb: And I have made Your name known to them and will continue to make it known, so that the love You have for Me may be in them, and I in them.”

verses.esv: When Jesus had spoken these words, he went out with his disciples across the brook Kidron, where there was a garden, which he and his disciples entered.
verses.kjv: WHEN Jesus had spoken these words, he went forth with his disciples over the brook Cedron, where was a garden, into the which he entered, and his disciples.
verses.bsb: After Jesus had spoken these words, He went out with His disciples across the Kidron Valley, where they entered a garden.

verses.esv: Now Judas, who betrayed him, also knew the place, for Jesus often met there with his disciples.
verses.kjv: And Judas also, which betrayed him, knew the place: for Jesus ofttimes resorted thither with his disciples.
verses.bsb: Now Judas His betrayer also knew the place, because Jesus had often met there with His disciples.

verses.esv: So Judas, having procured a band of soldiers and some officers from the chief priests and the Pharisees, went there with lanterns and torches and weapons.
verses.kjv: Judas then, having received a band of men and officers from the chief priests and Pharisees, cometh thither with lanterns and torches and weapons.
verses.bsb: So Judas brought a band of soldiers and officers from the chief priests and Pharisees. They arrived at the garden carrying lanterns, torches, and weapons.

verses.esv: Then Jesus, knowing all that would happen to him, came forward and said to them, “Whom do you seek?”
verses.kjv: Jesus therefore, knowing all things that should come upon him, went forth, and said unto them, Whom seek ye?
verses.bsb: Jesus, knowing all that was coming upon Him, stepped forward and asked them, “Whom are you seeking?”

verses.esv: They answered him, “Jesus of Nazareth.” Jesus said to them, “I am he.” Judas, who betrayed him, was standing with them.
verses.kjv: They answered him, Jesus of Nazareth. Jesus saith unto them, I am he. And Judas also, which betrayed him, stood with them.
verses.bsb: “Jesus of Nazareth,” they answered.  Jesus said, “I am He.”  And Judas His betrayer was standing there with them.

verses.esv: When Jesus said to them, “I am he,” they drew back and fell to the ground.
verses.kjv: As soon then as he had said unto them, I am he, they went backward, and fell to the ground.
verses.bsb: When Jesus said, “I am He,” they drew back and fell to the ground.

verses.esv: So he asked them again, “Whom do you seek?” And they said, “Jesus of Nazareth.”
verses.kjv: Then asked he them again, Whom seek ye? And they said, Jesus of Nazareth.
verses.bsb: So He asked them again, “Whom are you seeking?”  “Jesus of Nazareth,” they answered.

verses.esv: Jesus answered, “I told you that I am he. So, if you seek me, let these men go.”
verses.kjv: Jesus answered, I have told you that I am he: if therefore ye seek me, let these go their way:
verses.bsb: “I told you that I am He,” Jesus replied. “So if you are looking for Me, let these men go.”

verses.esv: This was to fulfill the word that he had spoken: “Of those whom you gave me I have lost not one.”
verses.kjv: That the saying might be fulfilled, which he spake, Of them which thou gavest me have I lost none.
verses.bsb: This was to fulfill the word He had spoken: “I have not lost one of those You have given Me.”

verses.esv: Then Simon Peter, having a sword, drew it and struck the high priest's servant and cut off his right ear. (The servant's name was Malchus.)
verses.kjv: Then Simon Peter having a sword drew it, and smote the high priest’s servant, and cut off his right ear. The servant’s name was Malchus.
verses.bsb: Then Simon Peter drew his sword and struck the servant of the high priest, cutting off his right ear. The servant’s name was Malchus.

verses.esv: So Jesus said to Peter, “Put your sword into its sheath; shall I not drink the cup that the Father has given me?”
verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto Peter, Put up thy sword into the sheath: the cup which my Father hath given me, shall I not drink it?
verses.bsb: “Put your sword back in its sheath!” Jesus said to Peter. “Shall I not drink the cup the Father has given Me?”

verses.esv: So the band of soldiers and their captain and the officers of the Jews arrested Jesus and bound him.
verses.kjv: Then the band and the captain and officers of the Jews took Jesus, and bound him,
verses.bsb: Then the band of soldiers, with its commander and the officers of the Jews, arrested Jesus and bound Him.

verses.esv: First they led him to Annas, for he was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, who was high priest that year.
verses.kjv: And led him away to Annas first; for he was father in law to Caiaphas, which was the high priest that same year.
verses.bsb: They brought Him first to Annas, who was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year.

verses.esv: It was Caiaphas who had advised the Jews that it would be expedient that one man should die for the people.
verses.kjv: Now Caiaphas was he, which gave counsel to the Jews, that it was expedient that one man should die for the people.
verses.bsb: Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jews that it would be better if one man died for the people.

verses.esv: Simon Peter followed Jesus, and so did another disciple. Since that disciple was known to the high priest, he entered with Jesus into the courtyard of the high priest,
verses.kjv: ¶ And Simon Peter followed Jesus, and so did another disciple: that disciple was known unto the high priest, and went in with Jesus into the palace of the high priest.
verses.bsb: Now Simon Peter and another disciple were following Jesus. Since that disciple was known to the high priest, he also went with Jesus into the courtyard of the high priest.

verses.esv: but Peter stood outside at the door. So the other disciple, who was known to the high priest, went out and spoke to the servant girl who kept watch at the door, and brought Peter in.
verses.kjv: But Peter stood at the door without. Then went out that other disciple, which was known unto the high priest, and spake unto her that kept the door, and brought in Peter.
verses.bsb: But Peter stood outside at the door. Then the disciple who was known to the high priest went out and spoke to the doorkeeper, and brought Peter in.

verses.esv: The servant girl at the door said to Peter, “You also are not one of this man's disciples, are you?” He said, “I am not.”
verses.kjv: Then saith the damsel that kept the door unto Peter, Art not thou also one of this man’s disciples? He saith, I am not.
verses.bsb: At this, the servant girl watching the door said to Peter, “Aren’t you also one of this man’s disciples?”  “I am not,” he answered.

verses.esv: Now the servants and officers had made a charcoal fire, because it was cold, and they were standing and warming themselves. Peter also was with them, standing and warming himself.
verses.kjv: And the servants and officers stood there, who had made a fire of coals; for it was cold: and they warmed themselves: and Peter stood with them, and warmed himself.
verses.bsb: Because it was cold, the servants and officers were standing around a charcoal fire they had made to keep warm. And Peter was also standing with them, warming himself.

verses.esv: The high priest then questioned Jesus about his disciples and his teaching.
verses.kjv: ¶ The high priest then asked Jesus of his disciples, and of his doctrine.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, the high priest questioned Jesus about His disciples and His teaching.

verses.esv: Jesus answered him, “I have spoken openly to the world. I have always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where all Jews come together. I have said nothing in secret.
verses.kjv: Jesus answered him, I spake openly to the world; I ever taught in the synagogue, and in the temple, whither the Jews always resort; and in secret have I said nothing.
verses.bsb: “I have spoken openly to the world,” Jesus answered. “I always taught in the synagogues and at the temple, where all the Jews come together. I said nothing in secret.

verses.esv: Why do you ask me? Ask those who have heard me what I said to them; they know what I said.”
verses.kjv: Why askest thou me? ask them which heard me, what I have said unto them: behold, they know what I said.
verses.bsb: Why are you asking Me? Ask those who heard My message. Surely they know what I said.”

verses.esv: When he had said these things, one of the officers standing by struck Jesus with his hand, saying, “Is that how you answer the high priest?”
verses.kjv: And when he had thus spoken, one of the officers which stood by struck Jesus with the palm of his hand, saying, Answerest thou the high priest so?
verses.bsb: When Jesus had said this, one of the officers standing nearby slapped Him in the face and said, “Is this how You answer the high priest?”

verses.esv: Jesus answered him, “If what I said is wrong, bear witness about the wrong; but if what I said is right, why do you strike me?”
verses.kjv: Jesus answered him, If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the evil: but if well, why smitest thou me?
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “If I said something wrong, testify as to what was wrong. But if I spoke correctly, why did you strike Me?”

verses.esv: Annas then sent him bound to Caiaphas the high priest.
verses.kjv: Now Annas had sent him bound unto Caiaphas the high priest.
verses.bsb: Then Annas sent Him, still bound, to Caiaphas the high priest.

verses.esv: Now Simon Peter was standing and warming himself. So they said to him, “You also are not one of his disciples, are you?” He denied it and said, “I am not.”
verses.kjv: And Simon Peter stood and warmed himself. They said therefore unto him, Art not thou also one of his disciples? He denied it, and said, I am not.
verses.bsb: Simon Peter was still standing and warming himself. So they asked him, “Aren’t you also one of His disciples?”  He denied it and said, “I am not.”

verses.esv: One of the servants of the high priest, a relative of the man whose ear Peter had cut off, asked, “Did I not see you in the garden with him?”
verses.kjv: One of the servants of the high priest, being his kinsman whose ear Peter cut off, saith, Did not I see thee in the garden with him?
verses.bsb: One of the high priest’s servants, a relative of the man whose ear Peter had cut off, asked, “Didn’t I see you with Him in the garden?”

verses.esv: Peter again denied it, and at once a rooster crowed.
verses.kjv: Peter then denied again: and immediately the cock crew.
verses.bsb: Peter denied it once more, and immediately a rooster crowed.

verses.esv: Then they led Jesus from the house of Caiaphas to the governor's headquarters. It was early morning. They themselves did not enter the governor's headquarters, so that they would not be defiled, but could eat the Passover.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then led they Jesus from Caiaphas unto the hall of judgment: and it was early; and they themselves went not into the judgment hall, lest they should be defiled; but that they might eat the passover.
verses.bsb: Then they led Jesus away from Caiaphas into the Praetorium. By now it was early morning, and the Jews did not enter the Praetorium, to avoid being defiled and unable to eat the Passover.

verses.esv: So Pilate went outside to them and said, “What accusation do you bring against this man?”
verses.kjv: Pilate then went out unto them, and said, What accusation bring ye against this man?
verses.bsb: So Pilate went out to them and asked, “What accusation are you bringing against this man?”

verses.esv: They answered him, “If this man were not doing evil, we would not have delivered him over to you.”
verses.kjv: They answered and said unto him, If he were not a malefactor, we would not have delivered him up unto thee.
verses.bsb: “If He were not a criminal,” they replied, “we would not have handed Him over to you.”

verses.esv: Pilate said to them, “Take him yourselves and judge him by your own law.” The Jews said to him, “It is not lawful for us to put anyone to death.”
verses.kjv: Then said Pilate unto them, Take ye him, and judge him according to your law. The Jews therefore said unto him, It is not lawful for us to put any man to death:
verses.bsb: “You take Him and judge Him by your own law,” Pilate told them.  “We are not permitted to execute anyone,” the Jews replied.

verses.esv: This was to fulfill the word that Jesus had spoken to show by what kind of death he was going to die.
verses.kjv: That the saying of Jesus might be fulfilled, which he spake, signifying what death he should die.
verses.bsb: This was to fulfill the word that Jesus had spoken to indicate the kind of death He was going to die.

verses.esv: So Pilate entered his headquarters again and called Jesus and said to him, “Are you the King of the Jews?”
verses.kjv: Then Pilate entered into the judgment hall again, and called Jesus, and said unto him, Art thou the King of the Jews?
verses.bsb: Pilate went back into the Praetorium, summoned Jesus, and asked Him, “Are You the King of the Jews?”

verses.esv: Jesus answered, “Do you say this of your own accord, or did others say it to you about me?”
verses.kjv: Jesus answered him, Sayest thou this thing of thyself, or did others tell it thee of me?
verses.bsb: “Are you saying this on your own,” Jesus asked, “or did others tell you about Me?”

verses.esv: Pilate answered, “Am I a Jew? Your own nation and the chief priests have delivered you over to me. What have you done?”
verses.kjv: Pilate answered, Am I a Jew? Thine own nation and the chief priests have delivered thee unto me: what hast thou done?
verses.bsb: “Am I a Jew?” Pilate replied. “Your own people and chief priests handed You over to me. What have You done?”

verses.esv: Jesus answered, “My kingdom is not of this world. If my kingdom were of this world, my servants would have been fighting, that I might not be delivered over to the Jews. But my kingdom is not from the world.”
verses.kjv: Jesus answered, My kingdom is not of this world: if my kingdom were of this world, then would my servants fight, that I should not be delivered to the Jews: but now is my kingdom not from hence.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “My kingdom is not of this world; if it were, My servants would fight to prevent My arrest by the Jews. But now My kingdom is not of this realm.”

verses.esv: Then Pilate said to him, “So you are a king?” Jesus answered, “You say that I am a king. For this purpose I was born and for this purpose I have come into the world—to bear witness to the truth. Everyone who is of the truth listens to my voice.”
verses.kjv: Pilate therefore said unto him, Art thou a king then? Jesus answered, Thou sayest that I am a king. To this end was I born, and for this cause came I into the world, that I should bear witness unto the truth. Every one that is of the truth heareth my voice.
verses.bsb: “Then You are a king!” Pilate said.  “You say that I am a king,” Jesus answered. “For this reason I was born and have come into the world, to testify to the truth. Everyone who belongs to the truth listens to My voice.”

verses.esv: Pilate said to him, “What is truth?” After he had said this, he went back outside to the Jews and told them, “I find no guilt in him.
verses.kjv: Pilate saith unto him, What is truth? And when he had said this, he went out again unto the Jews, and saith unto them, I find in him no fault at all.
verses.bsb: “What is truth?” Pilate asked.  And having said this, he went out again to the Jews and told them, “I find no basis for a charge against Him.

verses.esv: But you have a custom that I should release one man for you at the Passover. So do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?”
verses.kjv: But ye have a custom, that I should release unto you one at the passover: will ye therefore that I release unto you the King of the Jews?
verses.bsb: But it is your custom that I release to you one prisoner at the Passover. So then, do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?”

verses.esv: They cried out again, “Not this man, but Barabbas!” Now Barabbas was a robber.
verses.kjv: Then cried they all again, saying, Not this man, but Barabbas. Now Barabbas was a robber.
verses.bsb: “Not this man,” they shouted, “but Barabbas!” (Now Barabbas was an insurrectionist.)

verses.esv: Then Pilate took Jesus and flogged him.
verses.kjv: THEN Pilate therefore took Jesus, and scourged him.
verses.bsb: Then Pilate took Jesus and had Him flogged.

verses.esv: And the soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head and arrayed him in a purple robe.
verses.kjv: And the soldiers platted a crown of thorns, and put it on his head, and they put on him a purple robe,
verses.bsb: The soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns, set it on His head, and dressed Him in a purple robe.

verses.esv: They came up to him, saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!” and struck him with their hands.
verses.kjv: And said, Hail, King of the Jews! and they smote him with their hands.
verses.bsb: And they went up to Him again and again, saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!” and slapping Him in the face.

verses.esv: Pilate went out again and said to them, “See, I am bringing him out to you that you may know that I find no guilt in him.”
verses.kjv: Pilate therefore went forth again, and saith unto them, Behold, I bring him forth to you, that ye may know that I find no fault in him.
verses.bsb: Once again Pilate came out and said to the Jews, “Look, I am bringing Him out to you to let you know that I find no basis for a charge against Him.”

verses.esv: So Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe. Pilate said to them, “Behold the man!”
verses.kjv: Then came Jesus forth, wearing the crown of thorns, and the purple robe. And Pilate saith unto them, Behold the man!
verses.bsb: When Jesus came out wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe, Pilate said to them, “Here is the man!”

verses.esv: When the chief priests and the officers saw him, they cried out, “Crucify him, crucify him!” Pilate said to them, “Take him yourselves and crucify him, for I find no guilt in him.”
verses.kjv: When the chief priests therefore and officers saw him, they cried out, saying, Crucify him, crucify him. Pilate saith unto them, Take ye him, and crucify him: for I find no fault in him.
verses.bsb: As soon as the chief priests and officers saw Him, they shouted, “Crucify Him! Crucify Him!”  “You take Him and crucify Him,” Pilate replied, “for I find no basis for a charge against Him.”

verses.esv: The Jews answered him, “We have a law, and according to that law he ought to die because he has made himself the Son of God.”
verses.kjv: The Jews answered him, We have a law, and by our law he ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God.
verses.bsb: “We have a law,” answered the Jews, “and according to that law He must die, because He declared Himself to be the Son of God.”

verses.esv: When Pilate heard this statement, he was even more afraid.
verses.kjv: ¶ When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he was the more afraid;
verses.bsb: When Pilate heard this statement, he was even more afraid,

verses.esv: He entered his headquarters again and said to Jesus, “Where are you from?” But Jesus gave him no answer.
verses.kjv: And went again into the judgment hall, and saith unto Jesus, Whence art thou? But Jesus gave him no answer.
verses.bsb: and he went back into the Praetorium. “Where are You from?” he asked.  But Jesus gave no answer.

verses.esv: So Pilate said to him, “You will not speak to me? Do you not know that I have authority to release you and authority to crucify you?”
verses.kjv: Then saith Pilate unto him, Speakest thou not unto me? knowest thou not that I have power to crucify thee, and have power to release thee?
verses.bsb: So Pilate said to Him, “Do You refuse to speak to me? Do You not know that I have authority to release You and authority to crucify You?”

verses.esv: Jesus answered him, “You would have no authority over me at all unless it had been given you from above. Therefore he who delivered me over to you has the greater sin.”
verses.kjv: Jesus answered, Thou couldest have no power at all against me, except it were given thee from above: therefore he that delivered me unto thee hath the greater sin.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “You would have no authority over Me if it were not given to you from above. Therefore the one who handed Me over to you is guilty of greater sin.”

verses.esv: From then on Pilate sought to release him, but the Jews cried out, “If you release this man, you are not Caesar's friend. Everyone who makes himself a king opposes Caesar.”
verses.kjv: And from thenceforth Pilate sought to release him: but the Jews cried out, saying, If thou let this man go, thou art not Cæsar’s friend: whosoever maketh himself a king speaketh against Cæsar.
verses.bsb: From then on, Pilate tried to release Him, but the Jews kept shouting, “If you release this man, you are no friend of Caesar. Anyone who declares himself a king is defying Caesar.”

verses.esv: So when Pilate heard these words, he brought Jesus out and sat down on the judgment seat at a place called The Stone Pavement, and in Aramaic Gabbatha.
verses.kjv: ¶ When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he brought Jesus forth, and sat down in the judgment seat in a place that is called the Pavement, but in the Hebrew, Gabbatha.
verses.bsb: When Pilate heard these words, he brought Jesus out and sat on the judgment seat at a place called the Stone Pavement, which in Hebrew is Gabbatha.

verses.esv: Now it was the day of Preparation of the Passover. It was about the sixth hour. He said to the Jews, “Behold your King!”
verses.kjv: And it was the preparation of the passover, and about the sixth hour: and he saith unto the Jews, Behold your King!
verses.bsb: It was the day of Preparation for the Passover, about the sixth hour. And Pilate said to the Jews, “Here is your King!”

verses.esv: They cried out, “Away with him, away with him, crucify him!” Pilate said to them, “Shall I crucify your King?” The chief priests answered, “We have no king but Caesar.”
verses.kjv: But they cried out, Away with him, away with him, crucify him. Pilate saith unto them, Shall I crucify your King? The chief priests answered, We have no king but Cæsar.
verses.bsb: At this, they shouted, “Away with Him! Away with Him! Crucify Him!”  “Shall I crucify your King?” Pilate asked.  “We have no king but Caesar,” replied the chief priests.

verses.esv: So he delivered him over to them to be crucified. So they took Jesus,
verses.kjv: Then delivered he him therefore unto them to be crucified. And they took Jesus, and led him away.
verses.bsb: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be crucified, and the soldiers took Him away.

verses.esv: and he went out, bearing his own cross, to the place called The Place of a Skull, which in Aramaic is called Golgotha.
verses.kjv: And he bearing his cross went forth into a place called the place of a skull, which is called in the Hebrew Golgotha:
verses.bsb: Carrying His own cross, He went out to The Place of the Skull, which in Hebrew is called Golgotha.

verses.esv: There they crucified him, and with him two others, one on either side, and Jesus between them.
verses.kjv: Where they crucified him, and two other with him, on either side one, and Jesus in the midst.
verses.bsb: There they crucified Him, and with Him two others, one on each side, with Jesus in the middle.

verses.esv: Pilate also wrote an inscription and put it on the cross. It read, “Jesus of Nazareth, the King of the Jews.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And Pilate wrote a title, and put it on the cross. And the writing was, JESUS OF NAZARETH THE KING OF THE JEWS.
verses.bsb: Pilate also had a notice posted on the cross. It read:  JESUS OF NAZARETH,  THE KING OF THE JEWS.

verses.esv: Many of the Jews read this inscription, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city, and it was written in Aramaic, in Latin, and in Greek.
verses.kjv: This title then read many of the Jews: for the place where Jesus was crucified was nigh to the city: and it was written in Hebrew, and Greek, and Latin.
verses.bsb: Many of the Jews read this sign, because the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city, and it was written in Hebrew, Latin, and Greek.

verses.esv: So the chief priests of the Jews said to Pilate, “Do not write, ‘The King of the Jews,’ but rather, ‘This man said, I am King of the Jews.’”
verses.kjv: Then said the chief priests of the Jews to Pilate, Write not, The King of the Jews; but that he said, I am King of the Jews.
verses.bsb: So the chief priests of the Jews said to Pilate, “Do not write, ‘The King of the Jews,’ but only that He said, ‘I am the King of the Jews.’”

verses.esv: Pilate answered, “What I have written I have written.”
verses.kjv: Pilate answered, What I have written I have written.
verses.bsb: Pilate answered, “What I have written, I have written.”

verses.esv: When the soldiers had crucified Jesus, they took his garments and divided them into four parts, one part for each soldier; also his tunic. But the tunic was seamless, woven in one piece from top to bottom,
verses.kjv: ¶ Then the soldiers, when they had crucified Jesus, took his garments, and made four parts, to every soldier a part; and also his coat: now the coat was without seam, woven from the top throughout.
verses.bsb: When the soldiers had crucified Jesus, they divided His garments into four parts, one for each soldier, with the tunic remaining. It was seamless, woven in one piece from top to bottom.

verses.esv: so they said to one another, “Let us not tear it, but cast lots for it to see whose it shall be.” This was to fulfill the Scripture which says, “They divided my garments among them, and for my clothing they cast lots.” So the soldiers did these things,
verses.kjv: They said therefore among themselves, Let us not rend it, but cast lots for it, whose it shall be: that the scripture might be fulfilled, which saith, They parted my raiment among them, and for my vesture they did cast lots. These things therefore the soldiers did.
verses.bsb: So they said to one another, “Let us not tear it. Instead, let us cast lots to see who will get it.” This was to fulfill the Scripture:  “They divided My garments among them, and cast lots for My clothing.”   So that is what the soldiers did.

verses.esv: but standing by the cross of Jesus were his mother and his mother's sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now there stood by the cross of Jesus his mother, and his mother’s sister, Mary the wife of Cleophas, and Mary Magdalene.
verses.bsb: Near the cross of Jesus stood His mother and her sister, as well as Mary the wife of Clopas and Mary Magdalene.

verses.esv: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother, “Woman, behold, your son!”
verses.kjv: When Jesus therefore saw his mother, and the disciple standing by, whom he loved, he saith unto his mother, Woman, behold thy son!
verses.bsb: When Jesus saw His mother and the disciple whom He loved standing nearby, He said to His mother, “Woman, here is your son.”

verses.esv: Then he said to the disciple, “Behold, your mother!” And from that hour the disciple took her to his own home.
verses.kjv: Then saith he to the disciple, Behold thy mother! And from that hour that disciple took her unto his own home.
verses.bsb: Then He said to the disciple, “Here is your mother.” So from that hour, this disciple took her into his home.

verses.esv: After this, Jesus, knowing that all was now finished, said (to fulfill the Scripture), “I thirst.”
verses.kjv: ¶ After this, Jesus knowing that all things were now accomplished, that the scripture might be fulfilled, saith, I thirst.
verses.bsb: After this, knowing that everything had now been accomplished, and to fulfill the Scripture, Jesus said, “I am thirsty.”

verses.esv: A jar full of sour wine stood there, so they put a sponge full of the sour wine on a hyssop branch and held it to his mouth.
verses.kjv: Now there was set a vessel full of vinegar: and they filled a spunge with vinegar, and put it upon hyssop, and put it to his mouth.
verses.bsb: A jar of sour wine was sitting there. So they soaked a sponge in the wine, put it on a stalk of hyssop, and lifted it to His mouth.

verses.esv: When Jesus had received the sour wine, he said, “It is finished,” and he bowed his head and gave up his spirit.
verses.kjv: When Jesus therefore had received the vinegar, he said, It is finished: and he bowed his head, and gave up the ghost.
verses.bsb: When Jesus had received the sour wine, He said, “It is finished.” And bowing His head, He yielded up His spirit.

verses.esv: Since it was the day of Preparation, and so that the bodies would not remain on the cross on the Sabbath (for that Sabbath was a high day), the Jews asked Pilate that their legs might be broken and that they might be taken away.
verses.kjv: The Jews therefore, because it was the preparation, that the bodies should not remain upon the cross on the sabbath day, (for that sabbath day was an high day,) besought Pilate that their legs might be broken, and that they might be taken away.
verses.bsb: It was the day of Preparation, and the next day was a High Sabbath. In order that the bodies would not remain on the cross during the Sabbath, the Jews asked Pilate to have the legs broken and the bodies removed.

verses.esv: So the soldiers came and broke the legs of the first, and of the other who had been crucified with him.
verses.kjv: Then came the soldiers, and brake the legs of the first, and of the other which was crucified with him.
verses.bsb: So the soldiers came and broke the legs of the first man who had been crucified with Jesus, and those of the other.

verses.esv: But when they came to Jesus and saw that he was already dead, they did not break his legs.
verses.kjv: But when they came to Jesus, and saw that he was dead already, they brake not his legs:
verses.bsb: But when they came to Jesus and saw that He was already dead, they did not break His legs.

verses.esv: But one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and at once there came out blood and water.
verses.kjv: But one of the soldiers with a spear pierced his side, and forthwith came there out blood and water.
verses.bsb: Instead, one of the soldiers pierced His side with a spear, and immediately blood and water flowed out.

verses.esv: He who saw it has borne witness—his testimony is true, and he knows that he is telling the truth—that you also may believe.
verses.kjv: And he that saw it bare record, and his record is true: and he knoweth that he saith true, that ye might believe.
verses.bsb: The one who saw it has testified to this, and his testimony is true. He knows that he is telling the truth, so that you also may believe.

verses.esv: For these things took place that the Scripture might be fulfilled: “Not one of his bones will be broken.”
verses.kjv: For these things were done, that the scripture should be fulfilled, A bone of him shall not be broken.
verses.bsb: Now these things happened so that the Scripture would be fulfilled: “Not one of His bones will be broken.”

verses.esv: And again another Scripture says, “They will look on him whom they have pierced.”
verses.kjv: And again another scripture saith, They shall look on him whom they pierced.
verses.bsb: And, as another Scripture says: “They will look on the One they have pierced.”

verses.esv: After these things Joseph of Arimathea, who was a disciple of Jesus, but secretly for fear of the Jews, asked Pilate that he might take away the body of Jesus, and Pilate gave him permission. So he came and took away his body.
verses.kjv: ¶ And after this Joseph of Arimathæa, being a disciple of Jesus, but secretly for fear of the Jews, besought Pilate that he might take away the body of Jesus: and Pilate gave him leave. He came therefore, and took the body of Jesus.
verses.bsb: Afterward, Joseph of Arimathea, who was a disciple of Jesus (but secretly for fear of the Jews), asked Pilate to let him remove the body of Jesus. Pilate gave him permission, so he came and removed His body.

verses.esv: Nicodemus also, who earlier had come to Jesus by night, came bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about seventy-five pounds in weight.
verses.kjv: And there came also Nicodemus, which at the first came to Jesus by night, and brought a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about an hundred pound weight.
verses.bsb: Nicodemus, who had previously come to Jesus at night, also brought a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about seventy-five pounds.

verses.esv: So they took the body of Jesus and bound it in linen cloths with the spices, as is the burial custom of the Jews.
verses.kjv: Then took they the body of Jesus, and wound it in linen clothes with the spices, as the manner of the Jews is to bury.
verses.bsb: So they took the body of Jesus and wrapped it in linen cloths with the spices, according to the Jewish burial custom.

verses.esv: Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden, and in the garden a new tomb in which no one had yet been laid.
verses.kjv: Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden; and in the garden a new sepulchre, wherein was never man yet laid.
verses.bsb: Now there was a garden in the place where Jesus was crucified, and in the garden a new tomb in which no one had yet been laid.

verses.esv: So because of the Jewish day of Preparation, since the tomb was close at hand, they laid Jesus there.
verses.kjv: There laid they Jesus therefore because of the Jews’ preparation day; for the sepulchre was nigh at hand.
verses.bsb: And because it was the Jewish day of Preparation and the tomb was nearby, they placed Jesus there.

verses.esv: Now on the first day of the week Mary Magdalene came to the tomb early, while it was still dark, and saw that the stone had been taken away from the tomb.
verses.kjv: THE first day of the week cometh Mary Magdalene early, when it was yet dark, unto the sepulchre, and seeth the stone taken away from the sepulchre.
verses.bsb: Early on the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene went to the tomb and saw that the stone had been removed from the entrance.

verses.esv: So she ran and went to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said to them, “They have taken the Lord out of the tomb, and we do not know where they have laid him.”
verses.kjv: Then she runneth, and cometh to Simon Peter, and to the other disciple, whom Jesus loved, and saith unto them, They have taken away the Lord out of the sepulchre, and we know not where they have laid him.
verses.bsb: So she came running to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved. “They have taken the Lord out of the tomb,” she said, “and we do not know where they have put Him!”

verses.esv: So Peter went out with the other disciple, and they were going toward the tomb.
verses.kjv: Peter therefore went forth, and that other disciple, and came to the sepulchre.
verses.bsb: Then Peter and the other disciple set out for the tomb.

verses.esv: Both of them were running together, but the other disciple outran Peter and reached the tomb first.
verses.kjv: So they ran both together: and the other disciple did outrun Peter, and came first to the sepulchre.
verses.bsb: The two were running together, but the other disciple outran Peter and reached the tomb first.

verses.esv: And stooping to look in, he saw the linen cloths lying there, but he did not go in.
verses.kjv: And he stooping down, and looking in, saw the linen clothes lying; yet went he not in.
verses.bsb: He bent down and looked in at the linen cloths lying there, but he did not go in.

verses.esv: Then Simon Peter came, following him, and went into the tomb. He saw the linen cloths lying there,
verses.kjv: Then cometh Simon Peter following him, and went into the sepulchre, and seeth the linen clothes lie,
verses.bsb: Simon Peter arrived just after him. He entered the tomb and saw the linen cloths lying there.

verses.esv: and the face cloth, which had been on Jesus' head, not lying with the linen cloths but folded up in a place by itself.
verses.kjv: And the napkin, that was about his head, not lying with the linen clothes, but wrapped together in a place by itself.
verses.bsb: The cloth that had been around Jesus’ head was rolled up, lying separate from the linen cloths.

verses.esv: Then the other disciple, who had reached the tomb first, also went in, and he saw and believed;
verses.kjv: Then went in also that other disciple, which came first to the sepulchre, and he saw, and believed.
verses.bsb: Then the other disciple, who had reached the tomb first, also went in. And he saw and believed.

verses.esv: for as yet they did not understand the Scripture, that he must rise from the dead.
verses.kjv: For as yet they knew not the scripture, that he must rise again from the dead.
verses.bsb: For they still did not understand from the Scripture that Jesus had to rise from the dead.

verses.esv: Then the disciples went back to their homes.
verses.kjv: Then the disciples went away again unto their own home.
verses.bsb: Then the disciples returned to their homes.

verses.esv: But Mary stood weeping outside the tomb, and as she wept she stooped to look into the tomb.
verses.kjv: ¶ But Mary stood without at the sepulchre weeping: and as she wept, she stooped down, and looked into the sepulchre,
verses.bsb: But Mary stood outside the tomb weeping. And as she wept, she bent down to look into the tomb,

verses.esv: And she saw two angels in white, sitting where the body of Jesus had lain, one at the head and one at the feet.
verses.kjv: And seeth two angels in white sitting, the one at the head, and the other at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain.
verses.bsb: and she saw two angels in white sitting where the body of Jesus had lain, one at the head and the other at the feet.

verses.esv: They said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping?” She said to them, “They have taken away my Lord, and I do not know where they have laid him.”
verses.kjv: And they say unto her, Woman, why weepest thou? She saith unto them, Because they have taken away my Lord, and I know not where they have laid him.
verses.bsb: “Woman, why are you weeping?” they asked.  “Because they have taken my Lord away,” she said, “and I do not know where they have put Him.”

verses.esv: Having said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing, but she did not know that it was Jesus.
verses.kjv: And when she had thus said, she turned herself back, and saw Jesus standing, and knew not that it was Jesus.
verses.bsb: When she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there; but she did not recognize that it was Jesus.

verses.esv: Jesus said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping? Whom are you seeking?” Supposing him to be the gardener, she said to him, “Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have laid him, and I will take him away.”
verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, Woman, why weepest thou? whom seekest thou? She, supposing him to be the gardener, saith unto him, Sir, if thou have borne him hence, tell me where thou hast laid him, and I will take him away.
verses.bsb: “Woman, why are you weeping?” Jesus asked. “Whom are you seeking?”  Thinking He was the gardener, she said, “Sir, if you have carried Him off, tell me where you have put Him, and I will get Him.”

verses.esv: Jesus said to her, “Mary.” She turned and said to him in Aramaic, “Rabboni!” (which means Teacher).
verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, Mary. She turned herself, and saith unto him, Rabboni; which is to say, Master.
verses.bsb: Jesus said to her, “Mary.”  She turned and said to Him in Hebrew, “Rabboni!” (which means “Teacher”).

verses.esv: Jesus said to her, “Do not cling to me, for I have not yet ascended to the Father; but go to my brothers and say to them, ‘I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.’”
verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, Touch me not; for I am not yet ascended to my Father: but go to my brethren, and say unto them, I ascend unto my Father, and your Father; and to my God, and your God.
verses.bsb: “Do not cling to Me,” Jesus said, “for I have not yet ascended to the Father. But go and tell My brothers, ‘I am ascending to My Father and your Father, to My God and your God.’”

verses.esv: Mary Magdalene went and announced to the disciples, “I have seen the Lord”—and that he had said these things to her.
verses.kjv: Mary Magdalene came and told the disciples that she had seen the Lord, and that he had spoken these things unto her.
verses.bsb: Mary Magdalene went and announced to the disciples, “I have seen the Lord!” And she told them what He had said to her.

verses.esv: On the evening of that day, the first day of the week, the doors being locked where the disciples were for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood among them and said to them, “Peace be with you.”
verses.kjv: ¶ Then the same day at evening, being the first day of the week, when the doors were shut where the disciples were assembled for fear of the Jews, came Jesus and stood in the midst, and saith unto them, Peace be unto you.
verses.bsb: It was the first day of the week, and that very evening, while the disciples were together with the doors locked for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood among them. “Peace be with you!” He said to them.

verses.esv: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his side. Then the disciples were glad when they saw the Lord.
verses.kjv: And when he had so said, he shewed unto them his hands and his side. Then were the disciples glad, when they saw the Lord.
verses.bsb: After He had said this, He showed them His hands and His side.  The disciples rejoiced when they saw the Lord.

verses.esv: Jesus said to them again, “Peace be with you. As the Father has sent me, even so I am sending you.”
verses.kjv: Then said Jesus to them again, Peace be unto you: as my Father hath sent me, even so send I you.
verses.bsb: Again Jesus said to them, “Peace be with you. As the Father has sent Me, so also I am sending you.”

verses.esv: And when he had said this, he breathed on them and said to them, “Receive the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: And when he had said this, he breathed on them, and saith unto them, Receive ye the Holy Ghost:
verses.bsb: When He had said this, He breathed on them and said, “Receive the Holy Spirit.

verses.esv: If you forgive the sins of any, they are forgiven them; if you withhold forgiveness from any, it is withheld.”
verses.kjv: Whose soever sins ye remit, they are remitted unto them; and whose soever sins ye retain, they are retained.
verses.bsb: If you forgive anyone his sins, they are forgiven; if you withhold forgiveness from anyone, it is withheld.”

verses.esv: Now Thomas, one of the twelve, called the Twin, was not with them when Jesus came.
verses.kjv: ¶ But Thomas, one of the twelve, called Didymus, was not with them when Jesus came.
verses.bsb: Now Thomas called Didymus, one of the Twelve, was not with the disciples when Jesus came.

verses.esv: So the other disciples told him, “We have seen the Lord.” But he said to them, “Unless I see in his hands the mark of the nails, and place my finger into the mark of the nails, and place my hand into his side, I will never believe.”
verses.kjv: The other disciples therefore said unto him, We have seen the Lord. But he said unto them, Except I shall see in his hands the print of the nails, and put my finger into the print of the nails, and thrust my hand into his side, I will not believe.
verses.bsb: So the other disciples told him, “We have seen the Lord!”  But he replied, “Unless I see the nail marks in His hands, and put my finger where the nails have been, and put my hand into His side, I will never believe.”

verses.esv: Eight days later, his disciples were inside again, and Thomas was with them. Although the doors were locked, Jesus came and stood among them and said, “Peace be with you.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And after eight days again his disciples were within, and Thomas with them: then came Jesus, the doors being shut, and stood in the midst, and said, Peace be unto you.
verses.bsb: Eight days later, His disciples were once again inside with the doors locked, and Thomas was with them. Jesus came and stood among them and said, “Peace be with you.”

verses.esv: Then he said to Thomas, “Put your finger here, and see my hands; and put out your hand, and place it in my side. Do not disbelieve, but believe.”
verses.kjv: Then saith he to Thomas, Reach hither thy finger, and behold my hands; and reach hither thy hand, and thrust it into my side: and be not faithless, but believing.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to Thomas, “Put your finger here and look at My hands. Reach out your hand and put it into My side. Stop doubting and believe.”

verses.esv: Thomas answered him, “My Lord and my God!”
verses.kjv: And Thomas answered and said unto him, My Lord and my God.
verses.bsb: Thomas replied, “My Lord and my God!”

verses.esv: Jesus said to him, “Have you believed because you have seen me? Blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed.”
verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto him, Thomas, because thou hast seen me, thou hast believed: blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed.
verses.bsb: Jesus said to him, “Because you have seen Me, you have believed; blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed.”

verses.esv: Now Jesus did many other signs in the presence of the disciples, which are not written in this book;
verses.kjv: ¶ And many other signs truly did Jesus in the presence of his disciples, which are not written in this book:
verses.bsb: Jesus performed many other signs in the presence of His disciples, which are not written in this book.

verses.esv: but these are written so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that by believing you may have life in his name.
verses.kjv: But these are written, that ye might believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God; and that believing ye might have life through his name.
verses.bsb: But these are written so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that by believing you may have life in His name.

verses.esv: After this Jesus revealed himself again to the disciples by the Sea of Tiberias, and he revealed himself in this way.
verses.kjv: AFTER these things Jesus shewed himself again to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias; and on this wise shewed he himself.
verses.bsb: Later, by the Sea of Tiberias, Jesus again revealed Himself to the disciples. He made Himself known in this way:

verses.esv: Simon Peter, Thomas (called the Twin), Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, the sons of Zebedee, and two others of his disciples were together.
verses.kjv: There were together Simon Peter, and Thomas called Didymus, and Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, and the sons of Zebedee, and two other of his disciples.
verses.bsb: Simon Peter, Thomas called Didymus, Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, the sons of Zebedee, and two other disciples were together.

verses.esv: Simon Peter said to them, “I am going fishing.” They said to him, “We will go with you.” They went out and got into the boat, but that night they caught nothing.
verses.kjv: Simon Peter saith unto them, I go a fishing. They say unto him, We also go with thee. They went forth, and entered into a ship immediately; and that night they caught nothing.
verses.bsb: Simon Peter told them, “I am going fishing.”  “We will go with you,” they said. So they went out and got into the boat, but caught nothing that night.

verses.esv: Just as day was breaking, Jesus stood on the shore; yet the disciples did not know that it was Jesus.
verses.kjv: But when the morning was now come, Jesus stood on the shore: but the disciples knew not that it was Jesus.
verses.bsb: Early in the morning, Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples did not recognize that it was Jesus.

verses.esv: Jesus said to them, “Children, do you have any fish?” They answered him, “No.”
verses.kjv: Then Jesus saith unto them, Children, have ye any meat? They answered him, No.
verses.bsb: So He called out to them, “Children, do you have any fish?”  “No,” they answered.

verses.esv: He said to them, “Cast the net on the right side of the boat, and you will find some.” So they cast it, and now they were not able to haul it in, because of the quantity of fish.
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Cast the net on the right side of the ship, and ye shall find. They cast therefore, and now they were not able to draw it for the multitude of fishes.
verses.bsb: He told them, “Cast the net on the right side of the boat, and you will find some.” So they cast it there, and they were unable to haul it in because of the great number of fish.

verses.esv: That disciple whom Jesus loved therefore said to Peter, “It is the Lord!” When Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he put on his outer garment, for he was stripped for work, and threw himself into the sea.
verses.kjv: Therefore that disciple whom Jesus loved saith unto Peter, It is the Lord. Now when Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he girt his fisher’s coat unto him, (for he was naked,) and did cast himself into the sea.
verses.bsb: Then the disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, “It is the Lord!” As soon as Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he put on his outer garment (for he had removed it) and jumped into the sea.

verses.esv: The other disciples came in the boat, dragging the net full of fish, for they were not far from the land, but about a hundred yards off.
verses.kjv: And the other disciples came in a little ship; (for they were not far from land, but as it were two hundred cubits,) dragging the net with fishes.
verses.bsb: The other disciples came ashore in the boat. They dragged in the net full of fish, for they were not far from land, only about a hundred yards.

verses.esv: When they got out on land, they saw a charcoal fire in place, with fish laid out on it, and bread.
verses.kjv: As soon then as they were come to land, they saw a fire of coals there, and fish laid thereon, and bread.
verses.bsb: When they landed, they saw a charcoal fire there with fish on it, and some bread.

verses.esv: Jesus said to them, “Bring some of the fish that you have just caught.”
verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto them, Bring of the fish which ye have now caught.
verses.bsb: Jesus told them, “Bring some of the fish you have just caught.”

verses.esv: So Simon Peter went aboard and hauled the net ashore, full of large fish, 153 of them. And although there were so many, the net was not torn.
verses.kjv: Simon Peter went up, and drew the net to land full of great fishes, an hundred and fifty and three: and for all there were so many, yet was not the net broken.
verses.bsb: So Simon Peter went aboard and dragged the net ashore. It was full of large fish, 153, but even with so many, the net was not torn.

verses.esv: Jesus said to them, “Come and have breakfast.” Now none of the disciples dared ask him, “Who are you?” They knew it was the Lord.
verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto them, Come and dine. And none of the disciples durst ask him, Who art thou? knowing that it was the Lord.
verses.bsb: “Come, have breakfast,” Jesus said to them. None of the disciples dared to ask Him, “Who are You?” They knew it was the Lord.

verses.esv: Jesus came and took the bread and gave it to them, and so with the fish.
verses.kjv: Jesus then cometh, and taketh bread, and giveth them, and fish likewise.
verses.bsb: Jesus came and took the bread and gave it to them, and He did the same with the fish.

verses.esv: This was now the third time that Jesus was revealed to the disciples after he was raised from the dead.
verses.kjv: This is now the third time that Jesus shewed himself to his disciples, after that he was risen from the dead.
verses.bsb: This was now the third time that Jesus appeared to the disciples after He was raised from the dead.

verses.esv: When they had finished breakfast, Jesus said to Simon Peter, “Simon, son of John, do you love me more than these?” He said to him, “Yes, Lord; you know that I love you.” He said to him, “Feed my lambs.”
verses.kjv: ¶ So when they had dined, Jesus saith to Simon Peter, Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me more than these? He saith unto him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I love thee. He saith unto him, Feed my lambs.
verses.bsb: When they had finished eating, Jesus asked Simon Peter, “Simon son of John, do you love Me more than these?”  “Yes, Lord,” he answered, “You know I love You.”  Jesus replied, “Feed My lambs.”

verses.esv: He said to him a second time, “Simon, son of John, do you love me?” He said to him, “Yes, Lord; you know that I love you.” He said to him, “Tend my sheep.”
verses.kjv: He saith to him again the second time, Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me? He saith unto him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I love thee. He saith unto him, Feed my sheep.
verses.bsb: Jesus asked a second time, “Simon son of John, do you love Me?”  “Yes, Lord,” he answered, “You know I love You.”  Jesus told him, “Shepherd My sheep.”

verses.esv: He said to him the third time, “Simon, son of John, do you love me?” Peter was grieved because he said to him the third time, “Do you love me?” and he said to him, “Lord, you know everything; you know that I love you.” Jesus said to him, “Feed my sheep.
verses.kjv: He saith unto him the third time, Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me? Peter was grieved because he said unto him the third time, Lovest thou me? And he said unto him, Lord, thou knowest all things; thou knowest that I love thee. Jesus saith unto him, Feed my sheep.
verses.bsb: Jesus asked a third time, “Simon son of John, do you love Me?”  Peter was deeply hurt that Jesus had asked him a third time, “Do you love Me?”  “Lord, You know all things,” he replied. “You know I love You.”  Jesus said to him, “Feed My sheep.

verses.esv: Truly, truly, I say to you, when you were young, you used to dress yourself and walk wherever you wanted, but when you are old, you will stretch out your hands, and another will dress you and carry you where you do not want to go.”
verses.kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto thee, When thou wast young, thou girdedst thyself, and walkedst whither thou wouldest: but when thou shalt be old, thou shalt stretch forth thy hands, and another shall gird thee, and carry thee whither thou wouldest not.
verses.bsb: Truly, truly, I tell you, when you were young, you dressed yourself and walked where you wanted; but when you are old, you will stretch out your hands, and someone else will dress you and lead you where you do not want to go.”

verses.esv: (This he said to show by what kind of death he was to glorify God.) And after saying this he said to him, “Follow me.”
verses.kjv: This spake he, signifying by what death he should glorify God. And when he had spoken this, he saith unto him, Follow me.
verses.bsb: Jesus said this to indicate the kind of death by which Peter would glorify God.  And after He had said this, He told him, “Follow Me.”

verses.esv: Peter turned and saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following them, the one who also had leaned back against him during the supper and had said, “Lord, who is it that is going to betray you?”
verses.kjv: Then Peter, turning about, seeth the disciple whom Jesus loved following; which also leaned on his breast at supper, and said, Lord, which is he that betrayeth thee?
verses.bsb: Peter turned and saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following them. He was the one who had leaned back against Jesus at the supper to ask, “Lord, who is going to betray You?”

verses.esv: When Peter saw him, he said to Jesus, “Lord, what about this man?”
verses.kjv: Peter seeing him saith to Jesus, Lord, and what shall this man do?
verses.bsb: When Peter saw him, he asked, “Lord, what about him?”

verses.esv: Jesus said to him, “If it is my will that he remain until I come, what is that to you? You follow me!”
verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto him, If I will that he tarry till I come, what is that to thee? follow thou me.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “If I want him to remain until I return, what is that to you? You follow Me!”

verses.esv: So the saying spread abroad among the brothers that this disciple was not to die; yet Jesus did not say to him that he was not to die, but, “If it is my will that he remain until I come, what is that to you?”
verses.kjv: Then went this saying abroad among the brethren, that that disciple should not die: yet Jesus said not unto him, He shall not die; but, If I will that he tarry till I come, what is that to thee?
verses.bsb: Because of this, the rumor spread among the brothers that this disciple would not die. However, Jesus did not say that he would not die, but only, “If I want him to remain until I return, what is that to you?”

verses.esv: This is the disciple who is bearing witness about these things, and who has written these things, and we know that his testimony is true.
verses.kjv: This is the disciple which testifieth of these things, and wrote these things: and we know that his testimony is true.
verses.bsb: This is the disciple who testifies to these things and who has written them down. And we know that his testimony is true.

verses.esv: Now there are also many other things that Jesus did. Were every one of them to be written, I suppose that the world itself could not contain the books that would be written.
verses.kjv: And there are also many other things which Jesus did, the which, if they should be written every one, I suppose that even the world itself could not contain the books that should be written. Amen. THE
verses.bsb: There are many more things that Jesus did. If all of them were written down, I suppose that not even the world itself would have space for the books that would be written.
